# Train Mileage Status Grid - UX Specification

**Document ID:** MILEAGE-UX-004
**Version:** 1.0
**Created:** 2026-01-08
**Status:** Design Specification
**RFQ Reference:** Section 5 - Current maintenance status of all trains in terms of mileage

---

## 1. Executive Summary

The Train Mileage Status Grid is a **mission-critical component** displaying real-time status of all **128 trains** (49 Kinki Sharyo + 79 Alstom) in a data-dense, scannable format. This grid enables maintenance planners, fleet managers, and operations teams to quickly identify trains requiring attention, track PM windows, and monitor mileage balance across the fleet.

**Key Design Decisions:**
- **TanStack Table** for flexible, headless table logic
- **Virtual scrolling** for performance with full fleet view
- **25 rows per page** default with "Show All" option
- **Sticky headers + first column** for context during scroll
- **Status chips with colour coding** for instant visual scanning

---

## 2. User Requirements Analysis

### 2.1 Primary User Tasks

| Task | Frequency | Priority |
|------|-----------|----------|
| Identify trains approaching PM | Daily | P0 |
| Review fleet-wide mileage status | Daily | P0 |
| Find specific train by ID | Multiple/day | P0 |
| Filter by fleet type | Multiple/day | P1 |
| Sort by mileage remaining | Daily | P1 |
| Export data for reporting | Weekly | P2 |
| Bulk select for PM scheduling | Weekly | P2 |

### 2.2 Pain Points from Manual Excel Process (RFQ Background)
- Difficult to scan 128 rows for urgent items
- No real-time status updates
- Manual sorting/filtering is tedious
- Risk of missing overdue PM

---

## 3. Grid Architecture

### 3.1 Layout Overview (Gestalt: Enclosure + Continuity)

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Train Mileage Status                                     [Filter] [Export]  │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │  FILTERS BAR                                                            │ │
│  │  Fleet: [All ▼] Status: [All ▼] PM Due: [All ▼] Search: [________🔍]   │ │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                                                                              │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │  SUMMARY BAR                                                            │ │
│  │  Total: 128 │ FIT: 121 │ Maint: 5 │ OOS: 2 │ PM Due This Week: 4       │ │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                                                                              │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │  DATA GRID (Sticky Header)                                              │ │
│  ├────────┬───────────┬─────────┬──────────┬────────┬─────────┬──────────┤ │
│  │ □ │ID↓│ Name      │ Fleet   │ Mileage  │ Status │ Next PM │ Actions  │ │
│  ├────────┼───────────┼─────────┼──────────┼────────┼─────────┼──────────┤ │
│  │ □ │KS-001│Train 001│ Kinki S.│ 48,520   │ 🟢 FIT │ PM1 2d  │ [⋮]     │ │
│  │ □ │KS-002│Train 002│ Kinki S.│ 45,100   │ 🟢 FIT │ PM1 12d │ [⋮]     │ │
│  │ □ │KS-003│Train 003│ Kinki S.│ 49,850   │ 🟡 RST │ PM1 0d  │ [⋮]     │ │
│  │ □ │AL-001│Train 050│ Alstom  │ 47,200   │ 🟢 FIT │ PM1 7d  │ [⋮]     │ │
│  │ ...   │           │         │          │        │         │          │ │
│  └────────┴───────────┴─────────┴──────────┴────────┴─────────┴──────────┘ │
│                                                                              │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │  PAGINATION                                                             │ │
│  │  Showing 1-25 of 128  │ [◀] [1] [2] [3] [4] [5] [▶] │ Rows: [25 ▼]     │ │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                                                                              │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### 3.2 Column Specification

| Column | Width | Sortable | Filterable | Description |
|--------|-------|----------|------------|-------------|
| Checkbox | 40px | No | No | Bulk selection |
| Train ID | 80px | Yes | Yes | Unique identifier (KS-001, AL-001) |
| Name | 120px | Yes | Yes | Human-readable name |
| Fleet | 100px | Yes | Yes (dropdown) | Kinki Sharyo / Alstom |
| Current Mileage | 110px | Yes | Yes (range) | Current km since last overhaul |
| PM Window | 150px | Yes | Yes | Visual progress bar + percentage |
| Fitness Status | 100px | Yes | Yes (dropdown) | FIT / RESTRICTED / UNFIT |
| Train Status | 110px | Yes | Yes (dropdown) | IN_SERVICE / IN_MAINTENANCE / OOS |
| Next PM | 100px | Yes | Yes (range) | PM level + days remaining |
| Last Service | 100px | Yes | Yes (date range) | Date of last PM |
| Today's Assignment | 120px | No | Yes | Route ID or "Unassigned" |
| Actions | 60px | No | No | Context menu |

**Total minimum width:** ~1190px (horizontal scroll on smaller screens)

---

## 4. Visual Design System

### 4.1 Status Colour Coding (Gestalt: Similarity)

**Fitness Status:**
| Status | Chip Colour | Background | Text | Icon |
|--------|-------------|------------|------|------|
| FIT | Green | #DCFCE7 | #166534 | ✓ |
| RESTRICTED | Amber | #FEF3C7 | #92400E | ⚠ |
| UNFIT | Red | #FEE2E2 | #991B1B | ✕ |

**Train Status:**
| Status | Chip Colour | Background | Text |
|--------|-------------|------------|------|
| IN_SERVICE | Blue | #DBEAFE | #1E40AF |
| IN_MAINTENANCE | Purple | #EDE9FE | #5B21B6 |
| OUT_OF_SERVICE | Gray | #F3F4F6 | #374151 |

**PM Window Status (based on percentage to threshold):**
| Range | Background | Progress Bar | Text |
|-------|------------|--------------|------|
| 0-80% | #F0FDF4 | #22C55E (Green) | Safe |
| 80-90% | #FFFBEB | #F59E0B (Amber) | Approaching |
| 90-100% | #FEF2F2 | #EF4444 (Red) | Critical |
| >100% | #7F1D1D | Pulsing Red | OVERDUE |

### 4.2 PM Window Cell Design

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  PM1 @ 50,000 km                           │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │████████████████████░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░│   │ ← Progress bar (48,520/50,000)
│  └─────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│  97% │ 1,480 km remaining                  │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### 4.3 Row Styling

**Alternating Rows (Zebra Stripes):**
- Even rows: #FFFFFF
- Odd rows: #F9FAFB

**Row States:**
- Hover: #F3F4F6 background
- Selected: #DBEAFE background, blue left border
- Focus: 2px blue outline
- Critical (PM overdue): #FEE2E2 background, pulsing subtle

**Row Height:** 52px (optimal for touch + dense data)

---

## 5. Interaction Patterns

### 5.1 Sorting

**Single-Column Sort:**
```
[Train ID ↑] - Click header to sort
[Train ID ↓] - Click again to reverse
[Train ID]   - Click third time to clear
```

**Multi-Column Sort (Advanced):**
- Shift+Click to add secondary sort
- Display sort order indicators (1, 2, 3...)
- Clear all button in header

**Default Sort:** PM Window (Critical → Approaching → Safe)

### 5.2 Filtering

**Filter Bar Components:**

```
┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Fleet: [All ▼]  Status: [All ▼]  PM Due: [All ▼]  Search: [__________🔍]  │
│                                                                            │
│ Active Filters: [Kinki Sharyo ×] [PM Due: This Week ×]     [Clear All]    │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Fleet Filter:**
- All Fleets
- Kinki Sharyo (49)
- Alstom (79)

**Status Filter:**
- All Statuses
- FIT (121)
- RESTRICTED (5)
- UNFIT (2)

**PM Due Filter:**
- All
- Overdue (critical)
- This Week
- This Month
- Next 3 Months

**Search:**
- Real-time filtering as user types
- Searches: Train ID, Name
- Minimum 2 characters
- Debounced 300ms

### 5.3 Pagination

**Options:**
- 25 rows (default)
- 50 rows
- 100 rows
- Show All (with virtual scrolling)

**Controls:**
```
Showing 1-25 of 128 trains  │  [◀] [1] [2] [3] [4] [5] [▶]  │  Rows per page: [25 ▼]
```

**Behaviour:**
- Preserve sort/filter state across pages
- Jump to page via numbered buttons
- First/Last page shortcuts
- Keyboard navigation (←/→ arrows)

### 5.4 Row Actions Menu

**Click [⋮] to show:**
```
┌─────────────────────┐
│ 📋 View Details     │
│ 📅 Schedule PM      │
│ ✏️ Edit Assignment  │
│ 📊 Mileage History  │
│ ─────────────────── │
│ 🔗 Open in Maximo   │
└─────────────────────┘
```

### 5.5 Bulk Actions

**When rows selected:**
```
┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ 5 trains selected  │  [Schedule PM]  [Export]  [Clear]        │
└───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Select All:**
- Checkbox in header selects current page
- "Select all 128 matching" link appears after selecting page

### 5.6 Inline Editing (Optional - Phase 2)

- Double-click cell to edit (if permitted)
- Notes field editable inline
- Assignment editable via dropdown
- Save on blur or Enter

---

## 6. Responsive Design

### 6.1 Desktop (≥1440px)
- Full table view
- All columns visible
- Sticky first column + header
- Side-by-side filter controls

### 6.2 Laptop (1024px - 1439px)
- Horizontal scroll enabled
- Priority columns visible: ID, Mileage, PM Window, Status, Actions
- Other columns in horizontal scroll
- Sticky first two columns

### 6.3 Tablet (768px - 1023px)
- Card hybrid view option
- Essential columns only in table
- Expandable row for details
- Vertical filter stack

### 6.4 Mobile (<768px)
- **Card View** instead of table:

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────┐
│  KS-001                    🟢 FIT    │
│  Train 001 • Kinki Sharyo            │
├──────────────────────────────────────┤
│  Mileage: 48,520 km                  │
│  PM1 Window: ████████████░░░ 97%     │
│  Next PM: 2 days                      │
│  Assignment: RT-005                   │
├──────────────────────────────────────┤
│  [View Details] [Schedule PM]         │
└──────────────────────────────────────┘
```

---

## 7. Performance Considerations

### 7.1 Virtual Scrolling

For "Show All" mode with 128 rows:
- Use `react-window` or `@tanstack/virtual`
- Render only visible rows + buffer (10 rows above/below)
- Smooth 60fps scrolling
- Maintain row height consistency (52px)

### 7.2 Data Loading

| Scenario | Strategy |
|----------|----------|
| Initial load | Load first page (25 rows) + metadata |
| Pagination | Client-side (128 rows cacheable) |
| Filtering | Client-side real-time |
| Sorting | Client-side |
| Full export | Server-side generation |

### 7.3 Cache Strategy

- Cache full fleet data for 5 minutes
- WebSocket updates for status changes
- Optimistic UI for user actions
- Background refresh indicator

---

## 8. Accessibility (WCAG 2.1 AA)

### 8.1 Keyboard Navigation

| Key | Action |
|-----|--------|
| Tab | Move to next interactive element |
| Arrow Keys | Navigate cells (when focus in grid) |
| Space | Toggle checkbox / Expand row |
| Enter | Activate row action / Confirm |
| Escape | Close dropdown / Cancel |
| Home/End | First/Last row |
| Page Up/Down | Previous/Next page |

### 8.2 Screen Reader Support

- `role="grid"` on table container
- `role="row"` and `role="gridcell"` on rows/cells
- `aria-sort` on sortable headers
- `aria-selected` on selected rows
- `aria-label` on action buttons
- Live region for filter result count

### 8.3 Visual Accessibility

- All status colours meet 4.5:1 contrast ratio
- Icons + text (not colour alone) for status
- Focus visible on all interactive elements
- Reduced motion option for animations

---

## 9. Component Structure (React)

```tsx
<TrainMileageGrid>
  <GridToolbar>
    <FilterBar />
    <SearchInput />
    <ExportButton />
  </GridToolbar>

  <SummaryBar counts={fleetCounts} />

  <DataGrid>
    <GridHeader sticky>
      <SelectAllCheckbox />
      <SortableColumns />
    </GridHeader>

    <VirtualizedBody>
      {trains.map(train => (
        <GridRow key={train.id}>
          <SelectCheckbox />
          <TrainIdCell />
          <FleetBadge />
          <MileageCell />
          <PMWindowProgress />
          <StatusChip />
          <NextPMCell />
          <ActionsMenu />
        </GridRow>
      ))}
    </VirtualizedBody>
  </DataGrid>

  <BulkActionsBar selected={selectedCount} />

  <Pagination
    total={128}
    pageSize={25}
    currentPage={1}
  />
</TrainMileageGrid>
```

---

## 10. Wireframe: Full Desktop View

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Train Mileage Status                                                      [⚙] [↗]  │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                                     │
│ ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐│
│ │ Fleet        Status           PM Due            Search                          ││
│ │ ┌──────────┐ ┌──────────────┐ ┌──────────────┐  ┌────────────────────────────┐  ││
│ │ │All Fleets▼│ │All Statuses ▼│ │All          ▼│  │🔍 Search trains...         │  ││
│ │ └──────────┘ └──────────────┘ └──────────────┘  └────────────────────────────┘  ││
│ │                                                                                 ││
│ │ Active Filters: [Kinki Sharyo ✕]                               [Clear All]     ││
│ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘│

```

---

## 11. Unknown Unknowns Discovered

### 11.1 Data Integrity
1. **Stale mileage data** - What if daily feed is delayed? Show last-updated timestamp
2. **Conflicting sources** - Maximo vs. live feed discrepancy handling
3. **Manual corrections** - How to handle user-corrected mileage entries?

### 11.2 User Behaviour
4. **Default view preference** - Should default sort persist per user?
5. **Custom column order** - Allow users to reorder/hide columns?
6. **Saved filter presets** - Enable "My overdue trains" quick filter?

### 11.3 Edge Cases
7. **New trains added** - How to highlight newly added fleet members?
8. **Decommissioned trains** - Archive or hide? Historical data retention?
9. **PM level transitions** - When train passes PM1, immediately show PM2?

### 11.4 Performance
10. **Concurrent users** - 50+ users viewing same grid, update synchronisation
11. **Export large reports** - Background job for full 12-year projection export?
12. **Mobile offline** - Cache critical data for field access?

### 11.5 Business Logic
13. **PM scheduling authority** - Who can schedule PM from grid?
14. **Assignment override** - Can Operations override Maintenance recommendation?
15. **Compliance audit trail** - Log all status changes for audit?

---

## 12. Technology Recommendations

### 12.1 Core Dependencies

```json
{
  "@tanstack/react-table": "^8.x",
  "@tanstack/react-virtual": "^3.x",
  "date-fns": "^3.x",
  "clsx": "^2.x"
}
```

### 12.2 Performance Budget

| Metric | Target |
|--------|--------|
| Initial render (25 rows) | < 100ms |
| Sort operation | < 50ms |
| Filter operation | < 50ms |
| Virtual scroll FPS | 60fps |
| Bundle size (grid component) | < 50KB |

---

## 13. Sources & References

### Data Table UX
- [Pencil & Paper: Enterprise Data Tables UX Patterns](https://www.pencilandpaper.io/articles/ux-pattern-analysis-enterprise-data-tables)
- [UX World: Data Table Design Best Practices](https://uxdworld.com/data-table-design-best-practices/)
- [Justinmind: Designing Effective Data Table UI](https://www.justinmind.com/ui-design/data-table)
- [UX Planet: Best Practices for Data Tables](https://uxplanet.org/best-practices-for-usable-and-efficient-data-table-in-applications-4a1d1fb29550)
- [Stephanie Walter: Essential Resources for Complex Data Tables](https://stephaniewalter.design/blog/essential-resources-design-complex-data-tables/)

### Technical Implementation
- [Strapi: Table in React Performance Guide](https://strapi.io/blog/table-in-react-performance-guide)
- [TanStack Table Documentation](https://tanstack.com/table/latest)
- [Simple Table: TanStack vs AG Grid Comparison](https://www.simple-table.com/blog/tanstack-table-vs-ag-grid-comparison)

### Accessibility
- [W3C: Grid Pattern (WAI-ARIA)](https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/grid/)
- [Deque: Accessible Data Tables](https://www.deque.com/blog/accessible-data-tables/)

---

1→# Run Assignment Recommendation UI Specification
     2→
     3→**Document ID:** MILEAGE-UX-005
     4→**Version:** 1.0
     5→**Created:** 2026-01-08
     6→**RFQ Reference:** Sections 4.1.2, 5, 6
     7→
     8→---
     9→
    10→## 1. Overview
    11→
    12→### 1.1 Purpose
    13→
    14→This specification defines the user experience for the **Run Assignment Recommendation** interface, enabling operations staff to allocate trains to daily routes based on AI-optimized recommendations considering fitness status, mileage balance, and PM schedules.
    15→
    16→### 1.2 Target Users
    17→
    18→| Role | Primary Tasks | Device |
    19→|------|---------------|--------|
    20→| Operations Planner | Generate daily schedules, assign trains to routes | Desktop |
    21→| Depot Supervisor | Review assignments, approve changes | Desktop/Tablet |
    22→| Control Room | Monitor real-time assignments, handle exceptions | Desktop (multi-monitor) |
    23→| Maintenance Liaison | Coordinate train release/requests with operations | Desktop |
    24→
    25→### 1.3 RFQ Requirements Addressed
    26→
    27→- **Section 4.1.2:** "Recommends the most suitable train for a given run based on fitness status"
    28→- **Section 5:** "Train run assignments" in centralised dashboard
    29→- **Section 6:** "Daily operation requirements per train run ID"
    30→- **Section 5:** "Operations-Maintenance interface"
    31→
    32→---
    33→
    34→## 2. Information Architecture
    35→
    36→### 2.1 Screen Hierarchy
    37→
    38→```
    39→Run Assignment
    40→├── Daily Schedule View (Primary)
    41→│   ├── Date Selector
    42→│   ├── Fleet Overview Panel
    43→│   ├── Route List (Left)
    44→│   ├── Train Pool (Right)
    45→│   ├── Assignment Board (Center)
    46→│   └── Recommendations Panel
    47→├── Weekly Planner View
    48→│   ├── 7-Day Grid
    49→│   ├── Fleet Utilization Chart
    50→│   └── Mileage Balance Heatmap
    51→├── Mileage Deviation Report
    52→│   ├── Planned vs Actual Chart
    53→│   ├── Train-by-Train Analysis
    54→│   └── Trend Analysis
    55→└── Operations-Maintenance Messages
    56→    ├── Inbox
    57→    ├── Outbox
    58→    └── Compose Message
    59→```
    60→
    61→### 2.2 Navigation Pattern
    62→
    63→- **Tabs:** Daily | Weekly | Deviations | Messages
    64→- **Breadcrumb:** Run Assignment > Daily Schedule > [Date]
    65→- **Quick Actions:** Floating action button for common tasks
    66→

    68→

   113→
   114→### 3.2 Component Specifications
   115→
   116→#### 3.2.1 Fleet Overview Panel (Left Sidebar)
   117→
   118→**Purpose:** Quick status summary of entire fleet
   119→
   120→| Metric | Display | Color Coding |
   121→|--------|---------|--------------|
   122→| In Service | Count + progress bar | Green #22C55E |
   123→| In Maintenance | Count + progress bar | Amber #F59E0B |
   124→| Out of Service | Count + progress bar | Red #EF4444 |
   125→| Reserved | Count + progress bar | Blue #3B82F6 |
   126→
   127→**Interactions:**
   128→- Click metric to filter train pool
   129→- Hover shows breakdown by fleet type
   130→
   131→#### 3.2.2 Train Pool Panel
   132→
   133→**Purpose:** Draggable pool of available trains for assignment
   134→
   135→**Train Card (Compact):**
   136→```
   137→┌──────────────────────┐
   138→│ 🟢 KS-012           │
   139→│ 42,350 km | PM1 due │
   140→│ ▓▓▓▓▓▓▓░░░ 72%     │
   141→└──────────────────────┘
   142→```
   143→
   144→**Data Displayed:**
   145→- Train ID with fitness indicator (🟢 FIT, 🟡 RESTRICTED, 🔴 UNFIT)
   146→- Current mileage
   147→- Next PM level and progress
   148→- Visual progress to PM threshold
   149→
   150→**Filtering:**
   151→- Fleet type: Kinki Sharyo | Alstom | All
   152→- Fitness: FIT | RESTRICTED | All
   153→- PM Status: All | Due Soon | Safe
   154→
   155→**Sorting:**
   156→- By Mileage (Low to High) - default
   157→- By PM Progress (%)
   158→- By Train ID
   159→- By Score (for selected route)
   160→
   161→#### 3.2.3 Assignment Board (Center)
   162→
   163→**Purpose:** Main workspace for viewing and creating assignments
   164→
   165→**Route Row (Expanded):**
   166→```
   167→┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   168→│  RT-001  Express Line A                                        │
   169→│  ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━  │
   170→│  450 km | 06:00 - 22:00 | Priority: High | Requires: FIT       │
   171→├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
   172→│  ┌──────────────────────────────────────┐  ┌────────────────┐  │
   173→│  │  KS-001                    [✓][✕][⋮]│  │ Alternatives   │  │
   174→│  │  Score: 94 | Fitness: FIT           │  │ • KS-005 (91)  │  │
   175→│  │  Mileage: 38,500 → 38,950           │  │ • KS-012 (89)  │  │
   176→│  │  PM1 Due: 8 days                    │  │ [Show More]    │  │
   177→│  └──────────────────────────────────────┘  └────────────────┘  │
   178→│                                                                │

   181→```
   182→
   183→**States:**
   184→1. **Unassigned:** Shows empty drop zone with ghost outline
   185→2. **AI Suggested:** Shows suggested train with Accept/Reject buttons
   186→3. **Assigned:** Shows assigned train with Edit/Remove options
   187→4. **Locked:** Published assignment, cannot be modified
   188→
   189→**Visual Feedback:**
   190→- Drag hover: Blue outline #3B82F6, "Release to assign"
   191→- Invalid drop: Red outline #EF4444, "Fitness requirement not met"

   193→
   194→#### 3.2.4 Recommendations Panel (Right Sidebar)
   195→
   196→**Purpose:** AI-powered suggestions with transparency

   236→
   237→**Score Breakdown Drawer:**
   238→| Factor | Weight | Value | Score |
   239→|--------|--------|-------|-------|
   240→| Fitness Status | 30% | FIT (100) | 30 |
   241→| Mileage Balance | 40% | Low (85) | 34 |

   245→**Transparency Principles:**
   246→1. Always show WHY a train was recommended
   247→2. Show alternatives with trade-off explanation
   248→3. Allow users to override AI with clear action
   249→
   250→---
   251→
   252→## 4. AI Recommendation Patterns
   253→
   254→### 4.1 Suggestion States
   255→
   256→| State | Visual | Action Buttons |
   257→|-------|--------|----------------|
   258→| Pending Review | Yellow border, 🤖 icon | Accept, Reject |
   259→| Accepted | Green checkmark, fade animation | Undo (5s) |
   260→| Rejected | Strikethrough, gray | Select Alternative |
   261→| User Override | Blue border, 👤 icon | None (user chose) |
   262→
   263→### 4.2 Bulk Actions
   264→
   265→**Accept All Recommendations:**
   266→```

   520→## 9. Gestalt Principles Applied
   521→
   522→### 9.1 Proximity
   523→
   524→- **Fleet Overview:** Metrics grouped together in left sidebar
   525→- **Train Cards:** Related data (ID, mileage, PM) tightly grouped
   526→- **Recommendations:** Suggestion and alternatives in same panel
   527→
   528→### 9.2 Similarity
   529→
   530→- **Train Status:** Consistent color coding across all views
   531→  - 🟢 Green = FIT / Safe / Good
   532→  - 🟡 Yellow = RESTRICTED / Approaching / Fair
   533→  - 🔴 Red = UNFIT / Overdue / Poor
   534→- **Card Components:** Same border radius (8px), shadow (2px blur)
   535→
   536→### 9.3 Enclosure
   537→
   538→- **Route Slots:** Clear boundary for drop zones
   539→- **Message Cards:** Distinct containers for each message
   540→- **Panels:** Sidebar and main content clearly separated
   541→
   542→### 9.4 Continuity
   543→
   544→- **Weekly Grid:** Horizontal timeline reading left-to-right
   545→- **Score Breakdown:** Vertical list with progress bars aligned
   546→- **Fleet Balance:** Left-aligned labels, right-aligned values
   547→
   548→### 9.5 Figure-Ground
   549→
   550→- **Drag Preview:** Elevated shadow distinguishes dragged item
   551→- **Active Route:** Highlighted background during assignment
   552→- **Modal Dialogs:** Dimmed backdrop focuses attention
   553→
   554→### 9.6 Common Fate
   555→
   556→- **Multi-Select:** Selected items move/highlight together
   557→- **Route Groups:** Routes in same category collapse/expand together
   558→

   713→## 13. Performance Requirements
   714→
   715→### 13.1 Load Times
   716→
   717→| Metric | Target | Maximum |
   718→|--------|--------|---------|
   719→| Initial Page Load | <2s | 4s |
   720→| Route List Render | <500ms | 1s |
   721→| Train Pool Load (128 trains) | <300ms | 500ms |
   722→| AI Recommendation | <1s | 3s |
   723→| Drag-and-Drop Response | <50ms | 100ms |
   724→
   725→### 13.2 Optimization Strategies
   726→
   727→1. **Virtual scrolling** for train pool (>20 items)
   728→2. **Debounced search** (300ms delay)
   729→3. **Skeleton loading** for async data
   730→4. **Optimistic updates** for assignments
   731→5. **WebSocket** for real-time status updates
   732→
   733→---
   734→
   735→## 14. Unknown Unknowns Discovered
   736→
   737→### 14.1 Operational Challenges
   738→
   739→1. **Multi-day assignments:** Can a train be pre-assigned for multiple days? How to handle PM interruptions?
   740→2. **Split shifts:** Can one train serve multiple routes in a day? How to sequence?
   741→3. **Depot constraints:** If train is at Depot A, can it serve route starting at Depot B?
   742→4. **Crew dependencies:** Is crew assignment coupled with train assignment?
   743→5. **Fleet mixing rules:** Can Kinki Sharyo and Alstom be assigned interchangeably to all routes?
   744→
   745→### 14.2 UX Challenges
   746→
   747→6. **Undo granularity:** Undo single assignment vs undo all changes?
   748→7. **Auto-save vs manual save:** Should changes persist immediately or require explicit save?
   749→8. **Concurrent editing:** What if two planners modify the same day?
   750→9. **Historical edits:** Can past assignments be modified? Audit trail?
   751→10. **Template schedules:** Can users save assignment patterns as templates?
   752→
   753→### 14.3 AI/Algorithm Challenges
   754→
   755→11. **Score transparency:** How much algorithm detail to expose to users?
   756→12. **Override learning:** Should the system learn from user overrides to improve future suggestions?
   757→13. **Fairness constraints:** Should mileage be strictly balanced or allow temporary imbalances?
   758→14. **Look-ahead horizon:** Should AI consider tomorrow's schedule when suggesting today?
   759→15. **Constraint conflicts:** How to prioritize when fitness and mileage balance conflict?
   760→
   761→### 14.4 Integration Challenges
   762→
   763→16. **Maximo sync timing:** When are fitness statuses updated from Maximo?
   764→17. **Real-time mileage:** Is actual mileage available in real-time or end-of-day?
   765→18. **Schedule lock time:** When does a draft schedule become immutable?
   766→19. **Notification triggers:** Which changes should trigger Ops-Maint messages automatically?
   767→20. **Export format:** What format does the external scheduling system expect?
   768→
   769→---
   770→
   771→## 15. Sources & References
   772→
   773→### 15.1 Competitor Research
   774→
   775→- [CloudMoyo CCM](https://rail.cloudmoyo.com/crew-management-software-solution/) - Accept/reject via IVR and mobile
   776→- [Tracsis TRACS](https://tracsisops.com/software/operations-planning-software/tracs-scheduling-software) - Long-term/short-term planning modes
   777→- [DELMIA Rail Planning](https://www.3ds.com/products/delmia/logistics-workforce/rail-planning-operations) - Automatic validation of route/traction knowledge
   778→- [HASTUS (GIRO)](https://www.progressiverailroading.com/internet-digital/article/Software-providers-offer-ways-to-optimize-rail-crew-scheduling-rostering-and-recordkeeping--62872) - Work rules and fatigue management
   779→
   780→### 15.2 UX Patterns
   781→
   782→- [SAP Fiori AI Recommendations](https://www.sap.com/design-system/fiori-design-web/v1-120/foundations/ai-and-joule-design/guidelines/recommendations) - Human input wins, detect-explain-compare-act
   783→- [Nielsen Norman Group: Drag and Drop](https://www.nngroup.com/articles/drag-drop/) - Ease of use guidelines
   784→- [Smashing Magazine: Drag-Drop UX](https://smart-interface-design-patterns.com/articles/drag-and-drop-ux/) - Visual feedback states
   785→- [Hicron Fleet Management Dashboard](https://hicronsoftware.com/blog/fleet-management-dashboard-design/) - Role-based personalization
   786→
   787→### 15.3 Technical References
   788→
   789→- [react-dnd](https://react-dnd.github.io/react-dnd/about) - React drag-and-drop library
   790→- [dnd-kit](https://dndkit.com/) - Modern drag-and-drop toolkit
   791→- [Framer Motion](https://www.framer.com/motion/) - Animation library for React
   792→
   793→---
   794→
   795→## 16. Appendix: TypeScript Types Reference
   796→
   797→See `src/mileage/types/run-assignment.ts` for complete type definitions:
   798→- `Route` - Route/service definition
   799→- `RunAssignment` - Single train-route-day assignment
   800→- `DailySchedule` - All assignments for a day
   801→- `TrainRecommendation` - AI recommendation with score breakdown
   802→- `AssignmentOptimizationRequest` - Optimization input parameters
   803→- `AssignmentOptimizationResult` - Optimized assignments with metrics
   804→- `AssignmentWarning` - Warning types and severity
   805→- `MileageDeviation` - Planned vs actual deviation tracking
   806→- `FleetDailySummary` - Daily fleet status summary
   807→- `OpsMaintenanceMessage` - Operations-Maintenance communication
   808→
   809→---
   810→
   811→*Document Version: 1.0 | Last Updated: 2026-01-08*
   812→

# Run Assignment Recommendation UI Specification

**Document ID:** MILEAGE-UX-005
**Version:** 1.0
**Created:** 2026-01-08
**RFQ Reference:** Sections 4.1.2, 5, 6

---

## 1. Overview

### 1.1 Purpose

This specification defines the user experience for the **Run Assignment Recommendation** interface, enabling operations staff to allocate trains to daily routes based on AI-optimized recommendations considering fitness status, mileage balance, and PM schedules.

### 1.2 Target Users

| Role | Primary Tasks | Device |
|------|---------------|--------|
| Operations Planner | Generate daily schedules, assign trains to routes | Desktop |
| Depot Supervisor | Review assignments, approve changes | Desktop/Tablet |
| Control Room | Monitor real-time assignments, handle exceptions | Desktop (multi-monitor) |
| Maintenance Liaison | Coordinate train release/requests with operations | Desktop |

### 1.3 RFQ Requirements Addressed

- **Section 4.1.2:** "Recommends the most suitable train for a given run based on fitness status"
- **Section 5:** "Train run assignments" in centralised dashboard
- **Section 6:** "Daily operation requirements per train run ID"
- **Section 5:** "Operations-Maintenance interface"

---

## 2. Information Architecture

### 2.1 Screen Hierarchy

```
Run Assignment
├── Daily Schedule View (Primary)
│   ├── Date Selector
│   ├── Fleet Overview Panel
│   ├── Route List (Left)
│   ├── Train Pool (Right)
│   ├── Assignment Board (Center)
│   └── Recommendations Panel
├── Weekly Planner View
│   ├── 7-Day Grid
│   ├── Fleet Utilization Chart
│   └── Mileage Balance Heatmap
├── Mileage Deviation Report
│   ├── Planned vs Actual Chart
│   ├── Train-by-Train Analysis
│   └── Trend Analysis
└── Operations-Maintenance Messages
    ├── Inbox
    ├── Outbox
    └── Compose Message
```

### 2.2 Navigation Pattern

- **Tabs:** Daily | Weekly | Deviations | Messages
- **Breadcrumb:** Run Assignment > Daily Schedule > [Date]
- **Quick Actions:** Floating action button for common tasks

---

## 3. Daily Schedule View (Primary Screen)

### 3.1 Layout Structure (Desktop 1920x1080)

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  [Logo]  Run Assignment    [Daily] [Weekly] [Deviations] [Messages]   [?]   │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  ◀ Wed 08 Jan 2026 ▶    [Today]    Status: DRAFT ▼    [Auto-Assign] [Publish]│
├────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────┬───────────────────────┤
│ FLEET OVERVIEW │         ASSIGNMENT BOARD           │   RECOMMENDATIONS     │
│ ┌────────────┐ │  ┌──────────────────────────────┐  │  ┌─────────────────┐  │
│ │ In Service │ │  │   Route          Train      │  │  │ AI SUGGESTIONS  │  │
│ │    98      │ │  │ ┌────────────┬─────────────┐│  │  │                 │  │
│ │ ████████   │ │  │ │Express A   │ KS-001  ✓  ││  │  │ [Accept All]    │  │
│ └────────────┘ │  │ │RT-001      │ Score: 94  ││  │  │ [Review 3]      │  │
│ ┌────────────┐ │  │ └────────────┴─────────────┘│  │  │                 │  │
│ │In Maint.   │ │  │ ┌────────────┬─────────────┐│  │  │ RT-003: Shuttle │  │
│ │    22      │ │  │ │Local B     │ AL-015  ✓  ││  │  │ ┌─────────────┐ │  │
│ │ ████       │ │  │ │RT-002      │ Score: 88  ││  │  │ │ KS-012      │ │  │
│ └────────────┘ │  │ └────────────┴─────────────┘│  │  │ │ Score: 92   │ │  │
│ ┌────────────┐ │  │ ┌────────────┬─────────────┐│  │  │ │ [Accept]    │ │  │
│ │Out of Svc  │ │  │ │Shuttle     │ [Unassigned]││  │  │ └─────────────┘ │  │
│ │     8      │ │  │ │RT-003      │ Drag train ││  │  │ Alternatives:   │  │
│ │ █          │ │  │ └────────────┴─────────────┘│  │  │ • KS-015 (89)   │  │
│ └────────────┘ │  └──────────────────────────────┘  │  │ • AL-022 (86)   │  │
├────────────────┤                                    │  └─────────────────┘  │
│ TRAIN POOL     │  ┌──────────────────────────────┐  │                       │
│ ┌────────────┐ │  │  MILEAGE BALANCE TODAY       │  │  ┌─────────────────┐  │
│ │Available   │ │  │  ┌─────────────────────────┐ │  │  │ WARNINGS (3)    │  │
│ │   72       │ │  │  │ █████████░░░░░░░░░░░░░░│ │  │  │                 │  │
│ ├────────────┤ │  │  │ KS: 12,450 km          │ │  │  │ ⚠ AL-003 PM in  │  │
│ │Filter: All │ │  │  └─────────────────────────┘ │  │  │   2 days        │  │
│ │[KS][AL][▼] │ │  │  ┌─────────────────────────┐ │  │  │ ⚠ 3 routes need │  │
│ ├────────────┤ │  │  │ █████████████░░░░░░░░░░│ │  │  │   assignment    │  │
│ │ KS-005  🟢│ │  │  │ AL: 18,200 km          │ │  │  │ ⚠ Low reserve   │  │
│ │ KS-007  🟢│ │  │  └─────────────────────────┘ │  │  │   (4 trains)    │  │
│ │ KS-012  🟢│ │  └──────────────────────────────┘  │  └─────────────────┘  │
│ │ AL-008  🟢│ │                                    │                       │
│ │ AL-022  🟡│ │  Total: 30,650 km | Target: 32,000 │                       │
│ │ ...       │ │  Balance Score: 87/100             │                       │
│ └────────────┘ │                                    │                       │
└────────────────┴────────────────────────────────────┴───────────────────────┘
```

### 3.2 Component Specifications

#### 3.2.1 Fleet Overview Panel (Left Sidebar)

**Purpose:** Quick status summary of entire fleet

| Metric | Display | Color Coding |
|--------|---------|--------------|
| In Service | Count + progress bar | Green #22C55E |
| In Maintenance | Count + progress bar | Amber #F59E0B |
| Out of Service | Count + progress bar | Red #EF4444 |
| Reserved | Count + progress bar | Blue #3B82F6 |

**Interactions:**
- Click metric to filter train pool
- Hover shows breakdown by fleet type

#### 3.2.2 Train Pool Panel

**Purpose:** Draggable pool of available trains for assignment

**Train Card (Compact):**
```
┌──────────────────────┐
│ 🟢 KS-012           │
│ 42,350 km | PM1 due │
│ ▓▓▓▓▓▓▓░░░ 72%     │
└──────────────────────┘
```

**Data Displayed:**
- Train ID with fitness indicator (🟢 FIT, 🟡 RESTRICTED, 🔴 UNFIT)
- Current mileage
- Next PM level and progress
- Visual progress to PM threshold

**Filtering:**
- Fleet type: Kinki Sharyo | Alstom | All
- Fitness: FIT | RESTRICTED | All
- PM Status: All | Due Soon | Safe

**Sorting:**
- By Mileage (Low to High) - default
- By PM Progress (%)
- By Train ID
- By Score (for selected route)

#### 3.2.3 Assignment Board (Center)

**Purpose:** Main workspace for viewing and creating assignments

**Route Row (Expanded):**
```
┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  RT-001  Express Line A                                        │
│  ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━  │
│  450 km | 06:00 - 22:00 | Priority: High | Requires: FIT       │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  ┌──────────────────────────────────────┐  ┌────────────────┐  │
│  │  KS-001                    [✓][✕][⋮]│  │ Alternatives   │  │
│  │  Score: 94 | Fitness: FIT           │  │ • KS-005 (91)  │  │
│  │  Mileage: 38,500 → 38,950           │  │ • KS-012 (89)  │  │
│  │  PM1 Due: 8 days                    │  │ [Show More]    │  │
│  └──────────────────────────────────────┘  └────────────────┘  │
│                                                                │
│  Drop Zone: [     Drag train here or use AI suggestion     ]   │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**States:**
1. **Unassigned:** Shows empty drop zone with ghost outline
2. **AI Suggested:** Shows suggested train with Accept/Reject buttons
3. **Assigned:** Shows assigned train with Edit/Remove options
4. **Locked:** Published assignment, cannot be modified

**Visual Feedback:**
- Drag hover: Blue outline #3B82F6, "Release to assign"
- Invalid drop: Red outline #EF4444, "Fitness requirement not met"
- Successful drop: Green flash animation

#### 3.2.4 Recommendations Panel (Right Sidebar)

**Purpose:** AI-powered suggestions with transparency

**AI Suggestion Card:**
```
┌─────────────────────────────┐
│ 🤖 AI RECOMMENDATION        │
│                             │
│ Route: RT-003 Shuttle       │
│                             │
│ ┌─────────────────────────┐ │
│ │  KS-012                 │ │
│ │  ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ │ │
│ │  Score: 92/100          │ │
│ │                         │ │
│ │  Why this train?        │ │
│ │  ✓ Lowest mileage in    │ │
│ │    available pool       │ │
│ │  ✓ PM1 not due for      │ │
│ │    15 days              │ │
│ │  ✓ Matches route        │ │
│ │    fitness requirement  │ │
│ │                         │ │
│ │  [✓ Accept] [✕ Reject]  │ │
│ └─────────────────────────┘ │
│                             │
│ Alternatives (2):           │
│ ┌─────────────────────────┐ │
│ │ KS-015 | Score: 89      │ │
│ │ Higher mileage (+2.3k)  │ │
│ │ [Select]                │ │
│ └─────────────────────────┘ │
│ ┌─────────────────────────┐ │
│ │ AL-022 | Score: 86      │ │
│ │ Different fleet         │ │
│ │ [Select]                │ │
│ └─────────────────────────┘ │
│                             │
│ [Show Score Breakdown]      │
└─────────────────────────────┘
```

**Score Breakdown Drawer:**
| Factor | Weight | Value | Score |
|--------|--------|-------|-------|
| Fitness Status | 30% | FIT (100) | 30 |
| Mileage Balance | 40% | Low (85) | 34 |
| PM Distance | 30% | Safe (93) | 28 |
| **Total** | | | **92** |

**Transparency Principles:**
1. Always show WHY a train was recommended
2. Show alternatives with trade-off explanation
3. Allow users to override AI with clear action

---

## 4. AI Recommendation Patterns

### 4.1 Suggestion States

| State | Visual | Action Buttons |
|-------|--------|----------------|
| Pending Review | Yellow border, 🤖 icon | Accept, Reject |
| Accepted | Green checkmark, fade animation | Undo (5s) |
| Rejected | Strikethrough, gray | Select Alternative |
| User Override | Blue border, 👤 icon | None (user chose) |

### 4.2 Bulk Actions

**Accept All Recommendations:**
```
┌────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Accept 8 AI Recommendations?                  │
│                                                │
│  Summary:                                      │
│  • 5 Express routes assigned                   │
│  • 2 Local routes assigned                     │
│  • 1 Shuttle route assigned                    │
│                                                │
│  Fleet Balance: 87/100 (Good)                  │
│  Warnings: 1 (PM approaching for AL-003)       │
│                                                │
│  [Review Details]  [Cancel]  [Accept All]      │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### 4.3 Conflict Resolution

When user input conflicts with AI recommendation:

```
┌────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  ⚠️ Override AI Recommendation?                │
│                                                │
│  AI suggested: KS-012 (Score: 92)              │
│  You selected: KS-005 (Score: 78)              │
│                                                │
│  Trade-offs:                                   │
│  • KS-005 has higher mileage (+4,200 km)       │
│  • KS-005 PM due sooner (5 days vs 15 days)    │
│                                                │
│  [Keep AI] [Use My Selection]                  │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

---

## 5. Drag-and-Drop Interactions

### 5.1 Drag Source (Train Pool)

**Visual States:**
```
Default:        ┌─────────┐
                │ KS-012  │
                └─────────┘

Hover:          ┌─────────┐  ← cursor: grab
                │ KS-012  │     box-shadow: 0 4px 8px
                └─────────┘

Dragging:       ┌─────────┐  ← cursor: grabbing
                │ KS-012  │     opacity: 0.8
                └─────────┘     transform: rotate(2deg)

Ghost:          ┌ - - - - ┐  ← original position
                │ KS-012  │     opacity: 0.3
                └ - - - - ┘     dashed border
```

### 5.2 Drop Target (Route Slot)

**Visual States:**
```
Empty:          ┌──────────────────────────┐
                │  Drop train here         │
                │  ░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░ │
                └──────────────────────────┘

Drag Hover      ┌──────────────────────────┐
(Valid):        │  Release to assign       │  ← border: 2px solid #3B82F6
                │  KS-012                  │    background: #EFF6FF
                └──────────────────────────┘

Drag Hover      ┌──────────────────────────┐
(Invalid):      │  ✕ Requires FIT status   │  ← border: 2px solid #EF4444
                │  KS-012 is RESTRICTED    │    background: #FEF2F2
                └──────────────────────────┘

Assigned:       ┌──────────────────────────┐
                │  ✓ KS-012     [Edit][X]  │  ← border: none
                │  Score: 92 | FIT         │    background: white
                └──────────────────────────┘
```

### 5.3 Accessibility Requirements

- **Keyboard Alternative:** Tab to train, Enter to pick up, Arrow keys to navigate routes, Enter to drop
- **Screen Reader:** "KS-012, fit status, 42,350 kilometers, PM1 due in 15 days. Press Enter to pick up."
- **ARIA Attributes:** `aria-grabbed`, `aria-dropeffect`, live region for drop confirmation
- **Touch:** Long-press (300ms) to initiate drag, haptic feedback on grab/drop

---

## 6. Mileage Balance Visualization

### 6.1 Daily Balance Bar

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  MILEAGE BALANCE - Today                                     │
│                                                              │
│  Kinki Sharyo (49)                                           │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │████████████████████████████░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░│  │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│  Assigned: 12,450 km | Target: 16,000 km | Gap: -3,550 km    │
│                                                              │
│  Alstom (79)                                                 │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │█████████████████████████████████████░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░│  │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│  Assigned: 18,200 km | Target: 24,000 km | Gap: -5,800 km    │
│                                                              │
│  Overall Balance Score: 87/100 (Good)                        │
│  ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━  │
│  🟢 Excellent (95+) | 🟡 Good (80-94) | 🟠 Fair (60-79) | 🔴 Poor (<60)  │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### 6.2 Fleet Distribution Scatter

**Purpose:** Visualize mileage distribution across fleet to identify imbalances

```
  Mileage
    ▲
50k │                    ●        ● KS-003 (high)
    │           ●    ●     ●
45k │       ●●●   ●●●   ●●●
    │     ●●●●●●●●●●●●●
40k │   ●●●●●●●●●●●●●●●●●
    │  ●●●●●●●●●●●●●●●●●●●
35k │ ●●●●●●●●●●●●●●●●●●●●
    │●                    ● AL-042 (low)
30k │
    └────────────────────────────────▶
      KS-001  ...  KS-049  AL-001  ...  AL-079

      ● Kinki Sharyo    ● Alstom
      ── Target Range (±5%)
```

---

## 7. Weekly Planner View

### 7.1 7-Day Grid Layout

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Week: 06 Jan - 12 Jan 2026                      [◀ Prev] [Today] [Next ▶] │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│        │ Mon 06  │ Tue 07  │ Wed 08  │ Thu 09  │ Fri 10  │ Sat 11 │ Sun 12 │
├────────┼─────────┼─────────┼─────────┼─────────┼─────────┼────────┼────────┤
│Express │ KS-001  │ KS-001  │ KS-003  │ KS-003  │ KS-005  │ KS-007 │ KS-007 │
│RT-001  │ ✓       │ ✓       │ DRAFT   │ DRAFT   │ DRAFT   │ DRAFT  │ DRAFT  │
├────────┼─────────┼─────────┼─────────┼─────────┼─────────┼────────┼────────┤
│Local   │ AL-015  │ AL-015  │ AL-018  │ AL-018  │ AL-020  │ AL-020 │ AL-022 │
│RT-002  │ ✓       │ ✓       │ DRAFT   │ DRAFT   │ DRAFT   │ DRAFT  │ DRAFT  │
├────────┼─────────┼─────────┼─────────┼─────────┼─────────┼────────┼────────┤
│Shuttle │ KS-012  │ KS-012  │   ?     │   ?     │   ?     │   -    │   -    │

└────────┴─────────┴─────────┴─────────┴─────────┴─────────┴────────┴────────┘

Legend: ✓ Published | DRAFT | ? Unassigned | - Not Active | [Plan] Click to plan
```

### 7.2 Train Continuity View

**Purpose:** Track individual train assignment patterns

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  KS-001 - Week at a Glance                                      │
│                                                                 │
│  Mon    Tue    Wed    Thu    Fri    Sat    Sun                 │
│  ┌──┐   ┌──┐   ┌──┐   ┌──┐   ┌──┐   ┌──┐   ┌──┐                │
│  │░░│   │░░│   │░░│   │██│   │██│   │░░│   │░░│                │
│  │░░│   │░░│   │░░│   │██│   │██│   │░░│   │░░│                │
│  └──┘   └──┘   └──┘   └──┘   └──┘   └──┘   └──┘                │
│  RT-001 RT-001 RT-001  PM1   PM1   Rest   Rest                 │
│                                                                 │
│  Weekly Mileage: 1,350 km (Mon-Wed) + 0 km (Maintenance)       │
│  Status: In Maintenance Thu-Fri for PM1                        │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

░░ = In Service    ██ = Maintenance    ▓▓ = Out of Service
```

## 8. Operations-Maintenance Messaging

---

## 9. Gestalt Principles Applied

### 9.1 Proximity

- **Fleet Overview:** Metrics grouped together in left sidebar
- **Train Cards:** Related data (ID, mileage, PM) tightly grouped
- **Recommendations:** Suggestion and alternatives in same panel

### 9.2 Similarity

- **Train Status:** Consistent color coding across all views
  - 🟢 Green = FIT / Safe / Good
  - 🟡 Yellow = RESTRICTED / Approaching / Fair
  - 🔴 Red = UNFIT / Overdue / Poor
- **Card Components:** Same border radius (8px), shadow (2px blur)

### 9.3 Enclosure

- **Route Slots:** Clear boundary for drop zones
- **Message Cards:** Distinct containers for each message
- **Panels:** Sidebar and main content clearly separated

### 9.4 Continuity

- **Weekly Grid:** Horizontal timeline reading left-to-right
- **Score Breakdown:** Vertical list with progress bars aligned
- **Fleet Balance:** Left-aligned labels, right-aligned values

### 9.5 Figure-Ground

- **Drag Preview:** Elevated shadow distinguishes dragged item
- **Active Route:** Highlighted background during assignment
- **Modal Dialogs:** Dimmed backdrop focuses attention

### 9.6 Common Fate

- **Multi-Select:** Selected items move/highlight together
- **Route Groups:** Routes in same category collapse/expand together

---

## 12. Color System

### 12.1 Status Colors

| Status | Color | Hex | Usage |
|--------|-------|-----|-------|
| FIT / Success | Green | #22C55E | FIT trains, successful assignments |
| RESTRICTED / Warning | Amber | #F59E0B | RESTRICTED trains, approaching PM |
| UNFIT / Error | Red | #EF4444 | UNFIT trains, failed assignments |
| Info / AI | Blue | #3B82F6 | AI suggestions, information |
| Neutral | Gray | #6B7280 | Inactive, disabled states |

### 12.2 Score Colors

| Score Range | Color | Label |
|-------------|-------|-------|
| 95-100 | #22C55E | Excellent |
| 80-94 | #84CC16 | Good |
| 60-79 | #F59E0B | Fair |
| 40-59 | #F97316 | Poor |
| 0-39 | #EF4444 | Unsuitable |

### 12.3 Priority Colors

| Priority | Color | Background |
|----------|-------|------------|
| Urgent | #DC2626 | #FEF2F2 |
| High | #F97316 | #FFF7ED |
| Normal | #3B82F6 | #EFF6FF |
| Low | #6B7280 | #F9FAFB |

---

## 13. Performance Requirements

### 13.1 Load Times

| Metric | Target | Maximum |
|--------|--------|---------|
| Initial Page Load | <2s | 4s |
| Route List Render | <500ms | 1s |
| Train Pool Load (128 trains) | <300ms | 500ms |
| AI Recommendation | <1s | 3s |
| Drag-and-Drop Response | <50ms | 100ms |

### 13.2 Optimization Strategies

1. **Virtual scrolling** for train pool (>20 items)
2. **Debounced search** (300ms delay)
3. **Skeleton loading** for async data
4. **Optimistic updates** for assignments
5. **WebSocket** for real-time status updates

---

## 14. Unknown Unknowns Discovered

### 14.1 Operational Challenges

1. **Multi-day assignments:** Can a train be pre-assigned for multiple days? How to handle PM interruptions?
2. **Split shifts:** Can one train serve multiple routes in a day? How to sequence?
3. **Depot constraints:** If train is at Depot A, can it serve route starting at Depot B?
4. **Crew dependencies:** Is crew assignment coupled with train assignment?
5. **Fleet mixing rules:** Can Kinki Sharyo and Alstom be assigned interchangeably to all routes?

### 14.2 UX Challenges

6. **Undo granularity:** Undo single assignment vs undo all changes?
7. **Auto-save vs manual save:** Should changes persist immediately or require explicit save?
8. **Concurrent editing:** What if two planners modify the same day?
9. **Historical edits:** Can past assignments be modified? Audit trail?
10. **Template schedules:** Can users save assignment patterns as templates?

### 14.3 AI/Algorithm Challenges

11. **Score transparency:** How much algorithm detail to expose to users?
12. **Override learning:** Should the system learn from user overrides to improve future suggestions?
13. **Fairness constraints:** Should mileage be strictly balanced or allow temporary imbalances?
14. **Look-ahead horizon:** Should AI consider tomorrow's schedule when suggesting today?
15. **Constraint conflicts:** How to prioritize when fitness and mileage balance conflict?

### 14.4 Integration Challenges

16. **Maximo sync timing:** When are fitness statuses updated from Maximo?
17. **Real-time mileage:** Is actual mileage available in real-time or end-of-day?
18. **Schedule lock time:** When does a draft schedule become immutable?
19. **Notification triggers:** Which changes should trigger Ops-Maint messages automatically?
20. **Export format:** What format does the external scheduling system expect?

---

## 15. Sources & References

### 15.1 Competitor Research

- [CloudMoyo CCM](https://rail.cloudmoyo.com/crew-management-software-solution/) - Accept/reject via IVR and mobile
- [Tracsis TRACS](https://tracsisops.com/software/operations-planning-software/tracs-scheduling-software) - Long-term/short-term planning modes
- [DELMIA Rail Planning](https://www.3ds.com/products/delmia/logistics-workforce/rail-planning-operations) - Automatic validation of route/traction knowledge
- [HASTUS (GIRO)](https://www.progressiverailroading.com/internet-digital/article/Software-providers-offer-ways-to-optimize-rail-crew-scheduling-rostering-and-recordkeeping--62872) - Work rules and fatigue management

### 15.2 UX Patterns

- [SAP Fiori AI Recommendations](https://www.sap.com/design-system/fiori-design-web/v1-120/foundations/ai-and-joule-design/guidelines/recommendations) - Human input wins, detect-explain-compare-act
- [Nielsen Norman Group: Drag and Drop](https://www.nngroup.com/articles/drag-drop/) - Ease of use guidelines
- [Smashing Magazine: Drag-Drop UX](https://smart-interface-design-patterns.com/articles/drag-and-drop-ux/) - Visual feedback states
- [Hicron Fleet Management Dashboard](https://hicronsoftware.com/blog/fleet-management-dashboard-design/) - Role-based personalization

### 15.3 Technical References

- [react-dnd](https://react-dnd.github.io/react-dnd/about) - React drag-and-drop library
- [dnd-kit](https://dndkit.com/) - Modern drag-and-drop toolkit
- [Framer Motion](https://www.framer.com/motion/) - Animation library for React

---

## 16. Appendix: TypeScript Types Reference

See `src/mileage/types/run-assignment.ts` for complete type definitions:
- `Route` - Route/service definition
- `RunAssignment` - Single train-route-day assignment
- `DailySchedule` - All assignments for a day
- `TrainRecommendation` - AI recommendation with score breakdown
- `AssignmentOptimizationRequest` - Optimization input parameters
- `AssignmentOptimizationResult` - Optimized assignments with metrics
- `AssignmentWarning` - Warning types and severity
- `MileageDeviation` - Planned vs actual deviation tracking
- `FleetDailySummary` - Daily fleet status summary
- `OpsMaintenanceMessage` - Operations-Maintenance communication

---

content.content:
1→# Man-Hour Projection Chart - UX Design Specification
     2→
     3→**Task ID:** MILEAGE-UX-002
     4→**Status:** CRITICAL - RFQ Section 8 Case Study
     5→**Created:** 2026-01-08
     6→
     7→---
     8→
     9→## 1. Executive Summary
    10→
    11→The Man-Hour Projection Chart is the **core visualization** for resource planning in the Rolling Stock Mileage Management System. It displays workload (man-hours required) vs capacity (man-hours available) over a 12-year contract period, enabling planners to:
    12→
    13→1. Identify resource overallocation/underallocation
    14→2. Compare "Without adjustment" vs "With adjustment" scenarios
    15→3. Make data-driven decisions about maintenance scheduling
    16→
    17→**RFQ Reference:** Section 8 - Case Studies (Page 4)
    18→
    19→---
    20→
    21→## 2. Chart Type Selection
    22→
    23→### Primary Chart: Stacked Area/Line Combination Chart
    24→
    25→Based on RFQ Section 8 and [industry best practices](https://www.projectmanager.com/blog/what-is-a-resource-histogram):
    26→
    27→| Chart Element | Type | Purpose |
    28→|---------------|------|---------|
    29→| Availability (Capacity) | Solid Area | Shows available man-hours as baseline |
    30→| Adjusted Workload | Line with markers | Shows scheduled/leveled work |
    31→| Predicted Maintenance Workload | Dashed line | Shows forecasted demand before adjustment |
    32→
    33→### Why Not a Pure Histogram?
    34→- RFQ shows **continuous time series** (28+ months)
    35→- Need to show **three overlapping data series**
    36→- Gap analysis requires **line comparison** not stacked bars
    37→
    38→---
    39→
    40→## 3. Visual Specification
    41→
    42→### 3.1 Dimensions
    43→
    44→```
    45→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
    46→│  Man-Hour Projection                              [🔽 Timeline] │
    47→│  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  │
    48→│                                                                 │
    49→│  8000 ┤                                                         │
    50→│       │         ╭──╮                                            │
    51→│  6000 ┤    ╭───╯  ╰───╮      ╭─────╮                           │
    52→│       │   ╱            ╲    ╱       ╲                          │
    53→│  4000 ┤──╱──────────────╲──╱─────────╲───  ← Capacity Line     │
    54→│       │ ╱                ╲╱           ╲                        │
    55→│  2000 ┤╱   ████████████████████████████████  ← Availability    │
    56→│       │    (Shaded Area)                                       │
    57→│     0 ┼───┬───┬───┬───┬───┬───┬───┬───┬───┬───┬───┬───────────│
    58→│       Oct Nov Dec Jan Feb Mar Apr May Jun Jul Aug Sep ...      │
    59→│       '25 '25 '25 '26 '26 '26 '26 '26 '26 '26 '26 '26          │
    60→│                                                                 │
    61→│  ● Availability  ● Adjusted Workload  ○ Predicted Workload     │
    62→└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
    63→```
    64→
    65→### 3.2 Color Palette
    66→
    67→| Element | Color | Hex | Usage |
    68→|---------|-------|-----|-------|
    69→| Availability Area | Blue (muted) | `#3B82F6` opacity 30% | Capacity baseline |
    70→| Capacity Line | Blue (solid) | `#3B82F6` | Capacity threshold |
    71→| Adjusted Workload | Orange | `#F97316` | Scheduled work |
    72→| Predicted Workload | Gray dashed | `#6B7280` | Unleveled forecast |
    73→| Overallocation Zone | Red | `#EF4444` opacity 20% | Gap > 0 (demand > capacity) |
    74→| Underallocation Zone | Green | `#22C55E` opacity 20% | Gap < 0 (capacity > demand) |
    75→
    76→### 3.3 Data Labels
    77→
    78→- Show values on line markers when hovered
    79→- Show month labels on X-axis (abbreviated: Jan, Feb, Mar...)
    80→- Show year change markers (vertical dashed line)
    81→- Y-axis: Man-hours in increments of 1000 or auto-scaled
    82→
    83→---
    84→
    85→## 4. Data Table Component (Below Chart)
    86→
    87→Per RFQ Section 8, a data table **must** accompany the chart:
    88→
    89→```
    90→┌──────────┬────────┬────────┬────────┬────────┬────────┬────────┐
    91→│ Timeline │ Oct-25 │ Nov-25 │ Dec-25 │ Jan-26 │ Feb-26 │ ...    │
    92→├──────────┼────────┼────────┼────────┼────────┼────────┼────────┤
    93→│ No of Task│   55  │   64   │   62   │   45   │   57   │        │
    94→├──────────┼────────┼────────┼────────┼────────┼────────┼────────┤
    95→│ Maint.Work│  3706 │  3206  │  4201  │  2033  │  3042  │        │
    96→├──────────┼────────┼────────┼────────┼────────┼────────┼────────┤
    97→│ Adj.Work │  3564 │  2754  │  3990  │  2698  │  2306  │        │
    98→├──────────┼────────┼────────┼────────┼────────┼────────┼────────┤
    99→│ Avail(MH)│  3576 │  2929  │  3351  │  3729  │  2536  │        │
   100→├──────────┼────────┼────────┼────────┼────────┼────────┼────────┤
   101→│ Manpower │   19  │   15   │   22   │   22   │   22   │        │
   102→├──────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
   103→│ Gap(MH)  │   8   │  165   │ -639   │  1031  │  230   │ ...    │
   104→│          │  🟢   │  🟢    │  🔴    │  🟢    │  🟢    │        │
   105→└──────────┴────────┴────────┴────────┴────────┴────────┴────────┘
   106→```
   107→
   108→### Gap Row Highlighting Rules
   109→
   110→| Gap Value | Color | Icon | Meaning |
   111→|-----------|-------|------|---------|
   112→| Gap > 500 | Light Green | 🟢 | Comfortable capacity |
   113→| 0 < Gap ≤ 500 | Yellow | 🟡 | Near capacity |
   114→| -500 ≤ Gap < 0 | Orange | 🟠 | Slight overallocation |
   115→| Gap < -500 | Red | 🔴 | Critical overallocation |

   117→---
   118→
   119→## 5. Interaction Design
   120→
   121→### 5.1 Timeline Controls
   122→
   123→```
   124→[←] [Jan 2026 ▼] to [Dec 2027 ▼] [→]  [1M] [3M] [6M] [1Y] [ALL]
   125→```
   126→
   127→- **Date pickers**: Start/end month selection
   128→- **Quick ranges**: 1 month, 3 months, 6 months, 1 year, All (12 years)
   129→- **Arrow navigation**: Shift view by selected range
   130→
   131→### 5.2 Hover Interactions
   132→
   133→When hovering over a data point:
   134→```
   135→┌────────────────────────┐
   136→│ February 2026          │
   137→├────────────────────────┤
   138→│ Availability: 3,556 MH │
   139→│ Adjusted: 3,247 MH     │
   140→│ Predicted: 4,102 MH    │
   141→│ Gap: +309 MH           │

   144→```
   145→
   146→### 5.3 Click Actions
   147→
   148→- **Click on data point**: Opens detail panel for that month
   149→- **Click on Gap cell**: Opens adjustment wizard
   150→- **Double-click**: Zoom to selected month ± 3 months
   151→
   152→### 5.4 Comparison Mode Toggle
   153→

   156→```
   157→
   158→- **Without Adjustment**: Shows predicted workload without leveling
   159→- **With Adjustment**: Shows leveled workload (optimized)

   161→
   162→---
   163→
   164→## 6. Responsive Design
   165→
   166→### Desktop (≥1200px)
   167→- Full chart with data table below
   168→- All columns visible in table
   169→- Side panel for filters
   170→
   171→### Tablet (768px - 1199px)
   172→- Chart takes full width
   173→- Data table scrolls horizontally

   182→
   183→## 7. Accessibility
   184→
   185→### WCAG 2.1 AA Compliance
   186→
   187→| Requirement | Implementation |
   188→|-------------|----------------|
   189→| Color contrast | All text ≥ 4.5:1 ratio |
   190→| Non-color indicators | Patterns + icons for status |
   191→| Keyboard navigation | Tab through data points |
   192→| Screen reader | ARIA labels for all elements |
   193→| Focus indicators | Visible focus ring on interactive elements |
   194→
   195→### Alternative Views
   196→
   197→- **Table-only mode**: For screen reader users
   198→- **High contrast mode**: Black/white with patterns
   199→- **Data export**: CSV/Excel for external analysis
   200→
   201→---
   202→
   203→## 8. Data Model (TypeScript)
   204→
   205→```typescript
   206→// Chart data point for a single time period
   207→interface ManHourDataPoint {
   208→  period: {
   209→    year: number;
   210→    month: number;       // 1-12
   211→    label: string;       // "Jan-26"
   212→  };
   213→  taskCount: number;
   214→  predictedWorkload: number;    // Before adjustment
   215→  adjustedWorkload: number;     // After leveling
   216→  availableManHours: number;    // Capacity
   217→  availableManpower: number;    // Headcount
   218→  gap: number;                  // available - adjusted
   219→  utilizationPercent: number;   // (adjusted / available) * 100
   220→}
   221→
   222→// Chart configuration
   223→interface ManHourChartConfig {
   224→  timeRange: {
   225→    start: { year: number; month: number };
   226→    end: { year: number; month: number };
   227→  };
   228→  comparisonMode: 'without_adjustment' | 'with_adjustment' | 'side_by_side';
   229→  fleetFilter: 'ALL' | 'KINKI_SHARYO' | 'ALSTOM';
   230→  showDataTable: boolean;
   231→  showGapHighlighting: boolean;
   232→}
   233→
   234→// Chart state for UI
   235→interface ManHourChartState {
   236→  data: ManHourDataPoint[];
   237→  config: ManHourChartConfig;
   238→  hoveredPoint: ManHourDataPoint | null;
   239→  selectedPoint: ManHourDataPoint | null;
   240→  isLoading: boolean;
   241→  error: string | null;
   242→}
   243→
   244→// Gap severity classification
   245→type GapSeverity = 'comfortable' | 'near_capacity' | 'slight_over' | 'critical_over';
   246→
   247→function classifyGap(gap: number): GapSeverity {
   248→  if (gap > 500) return 'comfortable';
   249→  if (gap > 0) return 'near_capacity';
   250→  if (gap >= -500) return 'slight_over';
   251→  return 'critical_over';
   252→}
   253→```
   254→
   255→---
   256→
   257→## 9. Component Architecture
   258→
   259→```
   260→ManHourProjectionWidget/
   261→├── ManHourChart.tsx           # Main chart (recharts/visx)
   262→├── ManHourDataTable.tsx       # Data table below chart
   263→├── TimelineControls.tsx       # Date range selector
   264→├── ComparisonToggle.tsx       # Without/With adjustment toggle
   265→├── ChartTooltip.tsx           # Hover tooltip
   266→├── GapIndicator.tsx           # Gap cell with color coding
   267→├── ManHourDetailPanel.tsx     # Side panel for month details
   268→└── hooks/
   269→    ├── useManHourData.ts      # Data fetching hook
   270→    ├── useChartConfig.ts      # Configuration state
   271→    └── useGapAnalysis.ts      # Gap calculations
   272→```
   273→
   274→---
   275→
   276→## 10. Integration Points
   277→
   278→### Data Sources
   279→- `ResourceLevelerService.getProjection(timeRange)` - Get man-hour projections
   280→- `SimulationService.compareScenarios()` - Compare adjustment strategies
   281→
   282→### Events Emitted
   283→- `onPeriodSelect(period)` - When user selects a specific month
   284→- `onAdjustmentRequest(period)` - When user wants to adjust a month
   285→- `onExport(format)` - When user exports chart/data
   286→
   287→### Events Consumed
   288→- `maintenanceScheduleUpdated` - Refresh chart data
   289→- `resourceConfigChanged` - Recalculate availability
   290→
   291→---
   292→
   293→## 11. Performance Considerations
   294→
   295→| Scenario | Data Points | Strategy |
   296→|----------|-------------|----------|
   297→| 1 Year | 12 | Render all |
   298→| 5 Years | 60 | Render all |
   299→| 12 Years | 144 | Virtualize table, aggregate chart |
   300→
   301→### Optimization Strategies
   302→1. **Lazy load data table rows** beyond viewport
   303→2. **Aggregate to quarters** for views > 3 years
   304→3. **Debounce hover events** (100ms)
   305→4. **Memoize gap calculations**
   306→
   307→---
   308→
   309→## 12. Unknown Unknowns Discovered
   310→
   311→Through competitor research, several risks identified:
   312→
   313→| Risk | Impact | Mitigation |
   314→|------|--------|------------|
   315→| **Timezone issues** | Man-hours span midnight | Store in UTC, display in local |
   316→| **Holiday calendars** | Availability varies by country | Configurable working days |
   317→| **Overtime calculation** | Gap doesn't account for OT | Add "max overtime" parameter |
   318→| **Skill mismatch** | Man-hours available ≠ qualified | Add skill-based filtering |
   319→| **Multi-depot planning** | Each depot has different capacity | Depot-level breakdown view |
   320→| **Emergency maintenance** | Unplanned work disrupts schedule | Reserve buffer capacity |
   321→
   322→---
   323→
   324→## 13. Sources & References
   325→
   326→- [Resource Histogram Best Practices - ProjectManager.com](https://www.projectmanager.com/blog/what-is-a-resource-histogram)
   327→- [Capacity Planning Software - The Digital Project Manager](https://thedigitalprojectmanager.com/tools/best-capacity-planning-software/)

   331→- [Workload Management Tools - Runn](https://www.runn.io/blog/workload-management-tools)
   332→
   333→---
   334→

# Man-Hour Projection Chart - UX Design Specification

**Task ID:** MILEAGE-UX-002
**Status:** CRITICAL - RFQ Section 8 Case Study
**Created:** 2026-01-08

---

## 1. Executive Summary

The Man-Hour Projection Chart is the **core visualization** for resource planning in the Rolling Stock Mileage Management System. It displays workload (man-hours required) vs capacity (man-hours available) over a 12-year contract period, enabling planners to:

1. Identify resource overallocation/underallocation
2. Compare "Without adjustment" vs "With adjustment" scenarios
3. Make data-driven decisions about maintenance scheduling

**RFQ Reference:** Section 8 - Case Studies (Page 4)

---

## 2. Chart Type Selection

### Primary Chart: Stacked Area/Line Combination Chart

Based on RFQ Section 8 and [industry best practices](https://www.projectmanager.com/blog/what-is-a-resource-histogram):

| Chart Element | Type | Purpose |
|---------------|------|---------|
| Availability (Capacity) | Solid Area | Shows available man-hours as baseline |
| Adjusted Workload | Line with markers | Shows scheduled/leveled work |
| Predicted Maintenance Workload | Dashed line | Shows forecasted demand before adjustment |

### Why Not a Pure Histogram?
- RFQ shows **continuous time series** (28+ months)
- Need to show **three overlapping data series**
- Gap analysis requires **line comparison** not stacked bars

---

## 3. Visual Specification

### 3.1 Dimensions

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Man-Hour Projection                              [🔽 Timeline] │
│  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  │
│                                                                 │
│  8000 ┤                                                         │
│       │         ╭──╮                                            │
│  6000 ┤    ╭───╯  ╰───╮      ╭─────╮                           │
│       │   ╱            ╲    ╱       ╲                          │
│  4000 ┤──╱──────────────╲──╱─────────╲───  ← Capacity Line     │
│       │ ╱                ╲╱           ╲                        │
│  2000 ┤╱   ████████████████████████████████  ← Availability    │
│       │    (Shaded Area)                                       │
│     0 ┼───┬───┬───┬───┬───┬───┬───┬───┬───┬───┬───┬───────────│
│       Oct Nov Dec Jan Feb Mar Apr May Jun Jul Aug Sep ...      │
│       '25 '25 '25 '26 '26 '26 '26 '26 '26 '26 '26 '26          │
│                                                                 │
│  ● Availability  ● Adjusted Workload  ○ Predicted Workload     │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### 3.2 Color Palette

| Element | Color | Hex | Usage |
|---------|-------|-----|-------|
| Availability Area | Blue (muted) | `#3B82F6` opacity 30% | Capacity baseline |
| Capacity Line | Blue (solid) | `#3B82F6` | Capacity threshold |
| Adjusted Workload | Orange | `#F97316` | Scheduled work |
| Predicted Workload | Gray dashed | `#6B7280` | Unleveled forecast |
| Overallocation Zone | Red | `#EF4444` opacity 20% | Gap > 0 (demand > capacity) |
| Underallocation Zone | Green | `#22C55E` opacity 20% | Gap < 0 (capacity > demand) |

### 3.3 Data Labels

- Show values on line markers when hovered
- Show month labels on X-axis (abbreviated: Jan, Feb, Mar...)
- Show year change markers (vertical dashed line)
- Y-axis: Man-hours in increments of 1000 or auto-scaled

---

## 4. Data Table Component (Below Chart)

Per RFQ Section 8, a data table **must** accompany the chart:

```
┌──────────┬────────┬────────┬────────┬────────┬────────┬────────┐
│ Timeline │ Oct-25 │ Nov-25 │ Dec-25 │ Jan-26 │ Feb-26 │ ...    │
├──────────┼────────┼────────┼────────┼────────┼────────┼────────┤
│ No of Task│   55  │   64   │   62   │   45   │   57   │        │
├──────────┼────────┼────────┼────────┼────────┼────────┼────────┤
│ Maint.Work│  3706 │  3206  │  4201  │  2033  │  3042  │        │
├──────────┼────────┼────────┼────────┼────────┼────────┼────────┤
│ Adj.Work │  3564 │  2754  │  3990  │  2698  │  2306  │        │
├──────────┼────────┼────────┼────────┼────────┼────────┼────────┤
│ Avail(MH)│  3576 │  2929  │  3351  │  3729  │  2536  │        │
├──────────┼────────┼────────┼────────┼────────┼────────┼────────┤
│ Manpower │   19  │   15   │   22   │   22   │   22   │        │
├──────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Gap(MH)  │   8   │  165   │ -639   │  1031  │  230   │ ...    │
│          │  🟢   │  🟢    │  🔴    │  🟢    │  🟢    │        │
└──────────┴────────┴────────┴────────┴────────┴────────┴────────┘
```

### Gap Row Highlighting Rules

| Gap Value | Color | Icon | Meaning |
|-----------|-------|------|---------|
| Gap > 500 | Light Green | 🟢 | Comfortable capacity |
| 0 < Gap ≤ 500 | Yellow | 🟡 | Near capacity |
| -500 ≤ Gap < 0 | Orange | 🟠 | Slight overallocation |
| Gap < -500 | Red | 🔴 | Critical overallocation |

---

## 5. Interaction Design

### 5.1 Timeline Controls

```
[←] [Jan 2026 ▼] to [Dec 2027 ▼] [→]  [1M] [3M] [6M] [1Y] [ALL]
```

- **Date pickers**: Start/end month selection
- **Quick ranges**: 1 month, 3 months, 6 months, 1 year, All (12 years)
- **Arrow navigation**: Shift view by selected range

### 5.2 Hover Interactions

When hovering over a data point:
```
┌────────────────────────┐
│ February 2026          │
├────────────────────────┤
│ Availability: 3,556 MH │
│ Adjusted: 3,247 MH     │
│ Predicted: 4,102 MH    │
│ Gap: +309 MH           │
│ Utilization: 91.3%     │
└────────────────────────┘
```

### 5.3 Click Actions

- **Click on data point**: Opens detail panel for that month
- **Click on Gap cell**: Opens adjustment wizard
- **Double-click**: Zoom to selected month ± 3 months

### 5.4 Comparison Mode Toggle

```
[Without Adjustment] [With Adjustment] [Side-by-Side]
```

- **Without Adjustment**: Shows predicted workload without leveling
- **With Adjustment**: Shows leveled workload (optimized)
- **Side-by-Side**: Shows both charts stacked vertically

---

## 6. Responsive Design

### Desktop (≥1200px)
- Full chart with data table below
- All columns visible in table
- Side panel for filters

### Tablet (768px - 1199px)
- Chart takes full width
- Data table scrolls horizontally
- Filters in collapsible header

### Mobile (< 768px)
- Simplified chart (key metrics only)
- Data table replaced with card-based view
- Swipe to navigate months

---

## 7. Accessibility

### WCAG 2.1 AA Compliance

| Requirement | Implementation |
|-------------|----------------|
| Color contrast | All text ≥ 4.5:1 ratio |
| Non-color indicators | Patterns + icons for status |
| Keyboard navigation | Tab through data points |
| Screen reader | ARIA labels for all elements |
| Focus indicators | Visible focus ring on interactive elements |

### Alternative Views

- **Table-only mode**: For screen reader users
- **High contrast mode**: Black/white with patterns
- **Data export**: CSV/Excel for external analysis

---

## 8. Data Model (TypeScript)

```typescript
// Chart data point for a single time period
interface ManHourDataPoint {
  period: {
    year: number;
    month: number;       // 1-12
    label: string;       // "Jan-26"
  };
  taskCount: number;
  predictedWorkload: number;    // Before adjustment
  adjustedWorkload: number;     // After leveling
  availableManHours: number;    // Capacity
  availableManpower: number;    // Headcount
  gap: number;                  // available - adjusted
  utilizationPercent: number;   // (adjusted / available) * 100
}

// Chart configuration
interface ManHourChartConfig {
  timeRange: {
    start: { year: number; month: number };
    end: { year: number; month: number };
  };
  comparisonMode: 'without_adjustment' | 'with_adjustment' | 'side_by_side';
  fleetFilter: 'ALL' | 'KINKI_SHARYO' | 'ALSTOM';
  showDataTable: boolean;
  showGapHighlighting: boolean;
}

// Chart state for UI
interface ManHourChartState {
  data: ManHourDataPoint[];
  config: ManHourChartConfig;
  hoveredPoint: ManHourDataPoint | null;
  selectedPoint: ManHourDataPoint | null;
  isLoading: boolean;
  error: string | null;
}

// Gap severity classification
type GapSeverity = 'comfortable' | 'near_capacity' | 'slight_over' | 'critical_over';

function classifyGap(gap: number): GapSeverity {
  if (gap > 500) return 'comfortable';
  if (gap > 0) return 'near_capacity';
  if (gap >= -500) return 'slight_over';
  return 'critical_over';
}
```

---

## 9. Component Architecture

```
ManHourProjectionWidget/
├── ManHourChart.tsx           # Main chart (recharts/visx)
├── ManHourDataTable.tsx       # Data table below chart
├── TimelineControls.tsx       # Date range selector
├── ComparisonToggle.tsx       # Without/With adjustment toggle
├── ChartTooltip.tsx           # Hover tooltip
├── GapIndicator.tsx           # Gap cell with color coding
├── ManHourDetailPanel.tsx     # Side panel for month details
└── hooks/
    ├── useManHourData.ts      # Data fetching hook
    ├── useChartConfig.ts      # Configuration state
    └── useGapAnalysis.ts      # Gap calculations
```

---

## 10. Integration Points

### Data Sources
- `ResourceLevelerService.getProjection(timeRange)` - Get man-hour projections
- `SimulationService.compareScenarios()` - Compare adjustment strategies

### Events Emitted
- `onPeriodSelect(period)` - When user selects a specific month
- `onAdjustmentRequest(period)` - When user wants to adjust a month
- `onExport(format)` - When user exports chart/data

### Events Consumed
- `maintenanceScheduleUpdated` - Refresh chart data
- `resourceConfigChanged` - Recalculate availability

---

## 11. Performance Considerations

| Scenario | Data Points | Strategy |
|----------|-------------|----------|
| 1 Year | 12 | Render all |
| 5 Years | 60 | Render all |
| 12 Years | 144 | Virtualize table, aggregate chart |

### Optimization Strategies
1. **Lazy load data table rows** beyond viewport
2. **Aggregate to quarters** for views > 3 years
3. **Debounce hover events** (100ms)
4. **Memoize gap calculations**

---

## 12. Unknown Unknowns Discovered

Through competitor research, several risks identified:

| Risk | Impact | Mitigation |
|------|--------|------------|
| **Timezone issues** | Man-hours span midnight | Store in UTC, display in local |
| **Holiday calendars** | Availability varies by country | Configurable working days |
| **Overtime calculation** | Gap doesn't account for OT | Add "max overtime" parameter |
| **Skill mismatch** | Man-hours available ≠ qualified | Add skill-based filtering |
| **Multi-depot planning** | Each depot has different capacity | Depot-level breakdown view |
| **Emergency maintenance** | Unplanned work disrupts schedule | Reserve buffer capacity |

---

## 13. Sources & References

- [Resource Histogram Best Practices - ProjectManager.com](https://www.projectmanager.com/blog/what-is-a-resource-histogram)
- [Capacity Planning Software - The Digital Project Manager](https://thedigitalprojectmanager.com/tools/best-capacity-planning-software/)
- [Choosing Chart Types - Nielsen Norman Group](https://www.nngroup.com/articles/choosing-chart-types/)
- [Data Visualization Handbook - UX Magazine](https://uxmag.com/articles/the-ultimate-data-visualization-handbook-for-designers)
- [Stacked Bar Charts - Domo](https://www.domo.com/learn/charts/stacked-bar-charts)
- [Workload Management Tools - Runn](https://www.runn.io/blog/workload-management-tools)

---

## 14. Approval Checklist

- [ ] Reviewed by UX Designer
- [ ] Reviewed by Product Owner
- [ ] RFQ Section 8 compliance verified
- [ ] Accessibility audit passed
- [ ] Performance benchmarks met
- [ ] Approved for implementation
