1→# What-If Scenario / Simulation UI Specification
     2→
     3→**Document ID:** MILEAGE-UX-007
     4→**Version:** 1.0
     5→**Created:** 2026-01-08
     6→**RFQ Reference:** Sections 4.2, 5, 6, 8
     7→
     8→---
     9→
    10→## 1. Overview
    11→
    12→### 1.1 Purpose
    13→
    14→This specification defines the user experience for the **What-If Scenario / Simulation** module, enabling planners to model different mileage management strategies, compare outcomes, and make data-driven decisions about fleet utilization, maintenance scheduling, and resource allocation over the 12-year contract period.
    15→
    16→### 1.2 Target Users
    17→
    18→| Role | Primary Tasks | View Focus |
    19→|------|---------------|------------|
    20→| Maintenance Planner | Model PM schedule variations | Schedule Scenarios |
    21→| Operations Manager | Test run assignment strategies | Utilization Scenarios |
    22→| Fleet Manager | Balance mileage distribution | Fleet Strategy |
    23→| Finance Controller | Evaluate cost impact of decisions | Cost Projections |
    24→| Executive | Compare strategic options | Summary Comparison |
    25→
    26→### 1.3 RFQ Requirements Addressed
    27→
    28→- **Section 4.2:** "System shall have the ability to do what if scenarios"
    29→- **Section 5:** "Recommend and adjust maintenance plan based on simulation"
    30→- **Section 6:** "Mileage deviation tracking" and "10% underrun threshold"
    31→- **Section 8:** "Manpower planning with adjustment scenarios"
    32→
    33→### 1.4 Competitor Research Insights
    34→
    35→| Competitor | Key Feature | Applicable Insight |
    36→|------------|-------------|-------------------|
    37→| **DELMIA Quintiq** | Virtual Twin simulation | Full rail network modeling, scenario KPI comparison |
    38→| **Anaplan** | Multi-dimensional scenarios | Unlimited what-if models with instant comparison |
    39→| **Oracle EPM** | Scenario modeling capabilities | Best/worst/base case comparisons |
    40→| **SAP Analytics Cloud** | Driver-based forecasting | Parameter adjustment with rolling forecasts |
    41→| **AnyLogic** | Monte Carlo simulation | Probability distribution visualization |
    42→
    43→**Sources:**
    44→- [DELMIA Rail Planning](https://www.3ds.com/products/delmia/logistics-workforce/rail-planning-operations)
    45→- [Scenario Modeling Software Tools](https://www.farseer.com/blog/scenario-modeling-software/)
    46→- [Anaplan vs Oracle EPM Comparison](https://www.selecthub.com/epm-software/anaplan-vs-oracle-epm/)
    47→
    48→---
    49→
    50→## 2. Information Architecture
    51→
    52→### 2.1 Screen Hierarchy
    53→
    54→```
    55→What-If Scenarios
    56→├── Scenario Manager (Default)
    57→│   ├── Scenario List
    58→│   ├── Quick Create
    59→│   ├── Scenario Status
    60→│   └── Recent Comparisons
    61→├── Scenario Builder
    62→│   ├── Parameter Configuration
    63→│   ├── Strategy Selection

    65→│   └── Simulation Preview
    66→├── Scenario Comparison
    67→│   ├── Side-by-Side View
    68→│   ├── Overlay View
    69→│   ├── KPI Comparison Table
    70→│   └── Sensitivity Analysis
    71→├── Results Explorer
    72→│   ├── Timeline Projections
    73→│   ├── Resource Impact
    74→│   ├── Cost Projections
    75→│   └── Risk Analysis
    76→└── Saved Scenarios
    77→    ├── My Scenarios
    78→    ├── Team Scenarios
    79→    └── Published Templates
    80→```
    81→
    82→### 2.2 Navigation Pattern
    83→
    84→- **Tabs:** Manager | Builder | Compare | Results | Saved
    85→- **Breadcrumb:** Scenarios > [Scenario Name] > [View]
    86→- **Context Actions:** Clone, Compare, Export, Share
    87→
    88→---
    89→
    90→## 3. Scenario Manager View
    91→
    92→### 3.1 Layout Structure (Desktop 1920x1080)
    93→
    94→```
    95→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
    96→│  [Logo]  What-If Scenarios  [Manager] [Builder] [Compare] [Results] [Saved] │
    97→├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

    99→├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
   100→│  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
   101→│  │  QUICK START TEMPLATES                                                │  │
   102→│  │  ┌──────────────┐ ┌──────────────┐ ┌──────────────┐ ┌──────────────┐  │  │
   103→│  │  │ Aggressive   │ │ Conservative │ │ Balanced     │ │ Cost         │  │  │
   104→│  │  │ Mileage      │ │ Maintenance  │ │ Strategy     │ │ Optimized    │  │  │
   105→│  │  │              │ │              │ │              │ │              │  │  │
   106→│  │  │ High util    │ │ Early PM     │ │ Moderate     │ │ Min cost     │  │  │
   107→│  │  │ Max balance  │ │ Max safety   │ │ All factors  │ │ within SLA   │  │  │
   108→│  │  │ [Create →]   │ │ [Create →]   │ │ [Create →]   │ │ [Create →]   │  │  │
   109→│  │  └──────────────┘ └──────────────┘ └──────────────┘ └──────────────┘  │  │
   110→│  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
   111→├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
   112→│  MY SCENARIOS                                              Filter ▼  Sort ▼ │
   113→│  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
   114→│  │ □ │ Name                  │ Strategy  │ Status    │ Created   │ Actions│  │
   115→│  ├───┼───────────────────────┼───────────┼───────────┼───────────┼────────┤  │
   116→│  │ □ │ Q1 2026 Baseline      │ Balanced  │ ● Active  │ Jan 5     │ ⋮      │  │
   117→│  │ □ │ High Utilization Test │ Aggressive│ ○ Draft   │ Jan 7     │ ⋮      │  │
   118→│  │ □ │ PM Deferral Impact    │ Custom    │ ✓ Complete│ Jan 6     │ ⋮      │  │
   119→│  │ □ │ Summer Peak Strategy  │ Seasonal  │ ○ Draft   │ Jan 8     │ ⋮      │  │
   120→│  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
   121→├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
   122→│  RECENT COMPARISONS                                                          │

   124→│  │ Baseline vs High Utilization       │ │ Conservative vs Cost Optimized │  │
   125→│  │ Compared: 2 hours ago              │ │ Compared: Yesterday            │  │
   126→│  │ Result: High Util +8% efficiency   │ │ Result: Cost saves $120k/yr    │  │
   127→│  │ [View Comparison]                  │ │ [View Comparison]              │  │

   130→```
   131→
   132→### 3.2 Quick Start Templates
   133→
   134→| Template | Strategy | Description | Default Parameters |
   135→|----------|----------|-------------|-------------------|
   136→| **Aggressive Mileage** | Maximize utilization | Push trains to upper PM window | PM buffer: 5%, Tolerance: +10% |
   137→| **Conservative Maintenance** | Maximize safety | Early PM scheduling | PM buffer: 20%, Tolerance: -5% |
   138→| **Balanced Strategy** | Optimize all factors | Equal weight on metrics | PM buffer: 10%, Tolerance: 0% |
   139→| **Cost Optimized** | Minimize cost within SLA | Defer PM to latest safe point | PM buffer: 3%, Tolerance: +8% |
   140→| **Seasonal Peak** | Handle demand spikes | Higher utilization during peaks | Variable by month |
   141→| **Fleet Rebalance** | Equalize mileage | Target uniform distribution | Balance target: 98% |
   142→
   143→### 3.3 Scenario Status States
   144→
   145→| Status | Icon | Color | Description |
   146→|--------|------|-------|-------------|

   148→| Running | ◐ | Blue #3B82F6 | Simulation in progress |
   149→| Complete | ✓ | Green #22C55E | Results available |

   214→### 4.2 Parameter Slider Design
   215→
   216→Following Nielsen Norman Group's slider design guidelines:
   217→
   218→**Slider Design Requirements:**
   219→- **Thumb Size:** 32x32px with 44x44px touch target
   220→- **Track Width:** Full container width minus 32px padding
   221→- **Value Display:** Current value shown above slider thumb
   222→- **Step Indicators:** Tick marks at 25% intervals
   223→- **Real-Time Feedback:** Preview updates as slider moves
   224→- **Reset Button:** Small "↺" icon to restore default value
   225→
   226→```
   227→PM Window Buffer
   228→[↺]    5%
   229→├─────┼─────┼─────●─────┼─────┤
   230→0%   5%    10%   15%   20%   25%
   231→Early PM ←───────────────→ Late PM
   232→```
   233→
   234→**Sources:**
   235→- [NN/G Slider Design Rules of Thumb](https://www.nngroup.com/articles/gui-slider-controls/)
   236→- [Smashing Magazine Perfect Slider UX](https://www.smashingmagazine.com/2017/07/designing-perfect-slider/)
   237→
   238→### 4.3 Parameter Definitions
   239→
   240→| Parameter | Range | Default | Impact |
   241→|-----------|-------|---------|--------|
   242→| **PM Window Buffer** | 0-25% | 10% | How early before PM threshold to schedule |
   243→| **Mileage Tolerance** | -10% to +15% | 0% | Deviation from planned daily mileage |
   244→| **Fleet Balance Target** | 70-100% | 90% | How evenly distributed mileage should be |
   245→| **Man-Hours Available** | 50-100% | 90% | Resource capacity utilization |
   246→| **Workshop Tracks** | 1-6 | 4 | Concurrent PM capacity |
   247→| **Overtime Cap** | 0-30% | 15% | Maximum overtime percentage |
   248→
   249→### 4.4 Real-Time Preview Panel
   250→
   251→The preview panel updates within 500ms of parameter changes, showing:
   252→
   253→1. **Projected Outcomes:** KPI delta from baseline
   254→2. **Fleet Impact:** Progress bars by fleet type
   255→3. **Risk Indicators:** Warnings for constraint violations
   256→4. **Key Metrics:** 3-4 most relevant numbers
   257→

   323→### 5.3 Comparison Color Coding
   324→
   325→| Indicator | Symbol | Meaning |
   326→|-----------|--------|---------|
   327→| 🟢 Green | Better than baseline | Favorable outcome |
   328→| 🟡 Yellow | Within tolerance | Acceptable trade-off |
   329→| 🔴 Red | Worse than baseline | Unfavorable outcome |
   330→| ⬆ Arrow Up | Increase | Change direction |
   331→| ⬇ Arrow Down | Decrease | Change direction |

   333→### 5.4 Multi-Scenario Comparison (Up to 4)
   334→
   335→When comparing 3-4 scenarios, use a horizontal table with:
   336→- **Highlighted Winner:** Green background on best value per row
   337→- **Color Gradient:** Heat map coloring from best to worst
   338→- **Rank Indicator:** #1, #2, #3, #4 in corner of cells

   467→## 8. Gestalt Principles Applied
   468→
   469→### 8.1 Proximity
   470→
   471→- **Parameter Groups:** Related sliders grouped in labeled sections
   472→- **Comparison Cards:** Side-by-side scenarios with clear separation
   473→- **Action Buttons:** Primary actions grouped in toolbar
   474→
   475→### 8.2 Similarity
   476→
   477→- **Consistent Slider Design:** All parameter sliders have identical styling
   478→- **Status Colors:** Same color system across all views
   479→- **Card Layout:** Uniform card structure for scenarios
   480→
   481→### 8.3 Enclosure
   482→
   483→- **Panel Boundaries:** Clear card borders for each section
   484→- **Comparison Frames:** Distinct frames for each scenario being compared
   485→- **Result Groups:** Bordered sections for different metric categories
   486→
   487→### 8.4 Continuity
   488→
   489→- **Timeline Flow:** Left-to-right progression in projections
   490→- **Parameter Order:** Logical top-to-bottom priority ordering
   491→- **Visual Alignment:** Grid-based layout for comparison tables
   492→
   493→### 8.5 Figure-Ground
   494→
   495→- **Active Scenario:** Elevated shadow for selected scenario
   496→- **Hover States:** Subtle elevation on interactive elements
   497→- **Comparison Highlight:** Background tint on "winner" column
   498→
   499→### 8.6 Common Fate
   500→
   501→- **Linked Sliders:** Connected parameters animate together
   502→- **Comparison Sync:** Scroll position synced across compared scenarios
   503→- **Selection Propagation:** Multi-select actions apply uniformly
   504→

   507→## 9. Interaction Patterns
   508→
   509→### 9.1 Create New Scenario Flow
   510→
   511→```
   512→[Start] → Select Template → Adjust Parameters → Preview Results → [Save/Run]
   513→          (or Blank)       (real-time update)   (validate)
   514→```
   515→
   516→### 9.2 Compare Scenarios Flow
   517→
   518→```
   519→[Select 2-4 scenarios] → Choose View Mode → Analyze Differences → [Export/Apply]
   520→                         (Side/Overlay/Table)
   521→```
   522→
   523→### 9.3 Parameter Adjustment Interaction
   524→
   525→1. **Drag slider thumb** → Value updates in real-time
   526→2. **Click track** → Thumb jumps to click position
   527→3. **Keyboard arrows** → Increment/decrement by step
   528→4. **Type value** → Direct input in value field
   529→5. **Reset button** → Return to default value
   530→
   531→### 9.4 Real-Time Feedback Timing
   532→
   533→| Action | Response Time | Feedback Type |
   534→|--------|---------------|---------------|
   535→| Slider drag | < 100ms | Value label update |
   536→| Slider release | < 500ms | Preview panel update |
   537→| Run simulation | < 3s | Progress indicator |
   538→| Load comparison | < 1s | Side-by-side render |

   541→
   542→## 10. Responsive Design
   543→
   544→### 10.1 Desktop (≥1200px)
   545→
   546→- Full side-by-side comparison (2-4 scenarios)
   547→- Multi-column parameter layout
   548→- Expanded charts with full legends
   549→
   550→### 10.2 Tablet (768-1199px)
   551→
   552→- Stacked comparison (scroll between scenarios)
   553→- Single-column parameter layout
   554→- Condensed chart legends
   555→
   556→### 10.3 Mobile (<768px)
   557→
   558→- **Limited Functionality:** View results only, no scenario building
   559→- Swipe between scenarios
   560→- Collapsed parameter summary
   561→- Simplified chart views
   562→
   563→**Rationale:** Scenario building requires precise parameter adjustment that is difficult on small screens. Mobile users can review and compare existing scenarios but should use desktop/tablet for creation.
   564→
   565→---
   566→
   567→## 11. Accessibility Requirements
   568→
   569→### 11.1 Keyboard Navigation
   570→
   571→| Key | Action |
   572→|-----|--------|
   573→| Tab | Move between interactive elements |
   574→| Arrow Left/Right | Adjust slider value |
   575→| Enter | Confirm selection / Run simulation |
   576→| Escape | Cancel / Close modal |
   577→| Space | Toggle checkbox / Expand panel |
   578→
   579→### 11.2 Screen Reader Support
   580→
   581→- **ARIA Labels:** All sliders have descriptive labels
   582→- **Live Regions:** Result changes announced
   583→- **Value Announcements:** Slider values read on change
   584→
   585→### 11.3 Color Contrast
   586→
   587→- All text meets WCAG AA (4.5:1 minimum)
   588→- Status indicators have icon+text (not color alone)
   589→- Chart series differentiated by pattern + color
   590→
   591→---
   592→
   593→## 12. Unknown Unknowns Discovered
   594→
   595→Through research and analysis, the following "unknown unknowns" were identified:
   596→
   597→### 12.1 Simulation Complexity
   598→
   599→1. **Multi-year compounding:** Scenarios spanning 12 years have compounding effects that are hard to visualize
   600→2. **Interdependent parameters:** Changing one slider may require automatic adjustment of others
   601→3. **Stochastic vs deterministic:** Users may expect different randomness handling
   602→4. **Partial scenario runs:** What if simulation is stopped mid-way?
   603→
   604→### 12.2 User Collaboration
   605→
   606→5. **Concurrent editing:** What if two planners modify the same scenario?
   607→6. **Version history:** How to track changes to scenario parameters over time?
   608→7. **Approval workflow:** Should applying a scenario to live planning require approval?
   609→8. **Template ownership:** Who can create/modify shared templates?
   610→
   611→### 12.3 Data Quality
   612→
   613→9. **Input validation:** How to handle impossible parameter combinations?
   614→10. **Data freshness:** How recent must mileage data be for accurate simulation?
   615→11. **External factors:** How to model unplanned events (breakdowns, weather)?
   616→12. **Seasonal patterns:** Should scenarios automatically adjust for known peaks?
   617→
   618→### 12.4 Performance
   619→
   620→13. **Simulation time:** Long-horizon scenarios may take minutes - how to handle UX?
   621→14. **Cache invalidation:** When should cached results be recalculated?
   622→15. **Partial results:** Can users see preliminary results while simulation runs?
   623→16. **Resource limits:** How many concurrent simulations per user?
   624→
   625→### 12.5 Reporting Integration
   626→
   627→17. **Audit trail:** Must scenario decisions be logged for regulatory compliance?
   628→18. **Report embedding:** Can scenario results be embedded in external reports?
   629→19. **Data export format:** What formats do downstream systems require?
   630→20. **Historical comparison:** How long to retain old scenario results?
   631→
   632→---
   633→
   634→## 13. TypeScript Types Reference
   635→
   636→See: `src/mileage/types/scenario-ui.ts`
   637→
   638→Key types to be implemented:
   639→- `ScenarioStatus`, `ScenarioTemplate`, `ScenarioConfig`
   640→- `ScenarioParameter`, `ParameterSlider`, `ParameterConstraint`
   641→- `SimulationResult`, `SimulationMetrics`, `SimulationWarning`
   642→- `ComparisonView`, `ComparisonRow`, `ComparisonDelta`
   643→- `SensitivityAnalysis`, `TornadoChartData`, `MonteCarloDistribution`
   644→- `ScenarioBuilderState`, `ComparisonViewState`, `ResultsExplorerState`
   645→
   646→---
   647→
   648→## 14. Sources and References
   649→
   650→### Research Sources
   651→
   652→- [DELMIA Rail Planning Software](https://www.3ds.com/products/delmia/logistics-workforce/rail-planning-operations)
   653→- [DELMIA Virtual Twin for Rail Operations](https://blog.3ds.com/brands/delmia/transforming-rail-operations-for-a-smarter-future/)
   654→- [Scenario Modeling Software Tools](https://www.farseer.com/blog/scenario-modeling-software/)
   655→- [Anaplan vs Oracle EPM Comparison](https://www.selecthub.com/epm-software/anaplan-vs-oracle-epm/)
   656→- [Monte Carlo Simulation Dashboards](https://dev3lop.com/financial-risk-visualization-monte-carlo-simulation-dashboards/)
   657→- [Scenario Comparisons - Agentnoon](https://docs.agentnoon.com/scenarios/scenario-comparisons)
   658→
   659→### UX Design References
   660→
   661→- [NN/G Slider Design Guidelines](https://www.nngroup.com/articles/gui-slider-controls/)
   662→- [Smashing Magazine Perfect Slider UX](https://www.smashingmagazine.com/2017/07/designing-perfect-slider/)
   663→- [Dashboard UI/UX for Logistics](https://www.aufaitux.com/blog/dashboard-design-logistics-supply-chain-ux/)
   664→- [UX Strategies for Real-Time Dashboards](https://www.smashingmagazine.com/2025/09/ux-strategies-real-time-dashboards/)
   665→- [Visual Comparison Techniques](https://dev3lop.com/visual-comparison-techniques-for-before-after-analysis/)
   666→
   667→### Chart & Visualization References
   668→
   669→- [Metabase: Choosing the Right Visualization](https://www.metabase.com/blog/the-right-visualization)
   670→- [Types of Comparison Charts](https://chartexpo.com/blog/types-of-comparison-charts)
   671→- [Monte Carlo Charts in Practice](https://businessmap.io/blog/monte-carlo-chart-graph)
   672→
   673→---
   674→
   675→## 15. Revision History
   676→
   677→| Version | Date | Author | Changes |
   678→|---------|------|--------|---------|
   679→| 1.0 | 2026-01-08 | AI Assistant | Initial specification |
   680→

# What-If Scenario / Simulation UI Specification

**Document ID:** MILEAGE-UX-007
**Version:** 1.0
**Created:** 2026-01-08
**RFQ Reference:** Sections 4.2, 5, 6, 8

---

## 1. Overview

### 1.1 Purpose

This specification defines the user experience for the **What-If Scenario / Simulation** module, enabling planners to model different mileage management strategies, compare outcomes, and make data-driven decisions about fleet utilization, maintenance scheduling, and resource allocation over the 12-year contract period.

### 1.2 Target Users

| Role | Primary Tasks | View Focus |
|------|---------------|------------|
| Maintenance Planner | Model PM schedule variations | Schedule Scenarios |
| Operations Manager | Test run assignment strategies | Utilization Scenarios |
| Fleet Manager | Balance mileage distribution | Fleet Strategy |
| Finance Controller | Evaluate cost impact of decisions | Cost Projections |
| Executive | Compare strategic options | Summary Comparison |

### 1.3 RFQ Requirements Addressed

- **Section 4.2:** "System shall have the ability to do what if scenarios"
- **Section 5:** "Recommend and adjust maintenance plan based on simulation"
- **Section 6:** "Mileage deviation tracking" and "10% underrun threshold"
- **Section 8:** "Manpower planning with adjustment scenarios"

### 1.4 Competitor Research Insights

| Competitor | Key Feature | Applicable Insight |
|------------|-------------|-------------------|
| **DELMIA Quintiq** | Virtual Twin simulation | Full rail network modeling, scenario KPI comparison |
| **Anaplan** | Multi-dimensional scenarios | Unlimited what-if models with instant comparison |
| **Oracle EPM** | Scenario modeling capabilities | Best/worst/base case comparisons |
| **SAP Analytics Cloud** | Driver-based forecasting | Parameter adjustment with rolling forecasts |
| **AnyLogic** | Monte Carlo simulation | Probability distribution visualization |

**Sources:**
- [DELMIA Rail Planning](https://www.3ds.com/products/delmia/logistics-workforce/rail-planning-operations)
- [Scenario Modeling Software Tools](https://www.farseer.com/blog/scenario-modeling-software/)
- [Anaplan vs Oracle EPM Comparison](https://www.selecthub.com/epm-software/anaplan-vs-oracle-epm/)

---

## 2. Information Architecture

### 2.1 Screen Hierarchy

```
What-If Scenarios
├── Scenario Manager (Default)
│   ├── Scenario List
│   ├── Quick Create
│   ├── Scenario Status
│   └── Recent Comparisons
├── Scenario Builder
│   ├── Parameter Configuration
│   ├── Strategy Selection
│   ├── Constraint Settings
│   └── Simulation Preview
├── Scenario Comparison
│   ├── Side-by-Side View
│   ├── Overlay View
│   ├── KPI Comparison Table
│   └── Sensitivity Analysis
├── Results Explorer
│   ├── Timeline Projections
│   ├── Resource Impact
│   ├── Cost Projections
│   └── Risk Analysis
└── Saved Scenarios
    ├── My Scenarios
    ├── Team Scenarios
    └── Published Templates
```

### 2.2 Navigation Pattern

- **Tabs:** Manager | Builder | Compare | Results | Saved
- **Breadcrumb:** Scenarios > [Scenario Name] > [View]
- **Context Actions:** Clone, Compare, Export, Share

---

## 3. Scenario Manager View

### 3.1 Layout Structure (Desktop 1920x1080)

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  [Logo]  What-If Scenarios  [Manager] [Builder] [Compare] [Results] [Saved] │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  [+ New Scenario]  [Clone Selected]  [Compare Selected]     [⚙ Settings]    │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │  QUICK START TEMPLATES                                                │  │
│  │  ┌──────────────┐ ┌──────────────┐ ┌──────────────┐ ┌──────────────┐  │  │
│  │  │ Aggressive   │ │ Conservative │ │ Balanced     │ │ Cost         │  │  │
│  │  │ Mileage      │ │ Maintenance  │ │ Strategy     │ │ Optimized    │  │  │
│  │  │              │ │              │ │              │ │              │  │  │
│  │  │ High util    │ │ Early PM     │ │ Moderate     │ │ Min cost     │  │  │
│  │  │ Max balance  │ │ Max safety   │ │ All factors  │ │ within SLA   │  │  │
│  │  │ [Create →]   │ │ [Create →]   │ │ [Create →]   │ │ [Create →]   │  │  │
│  │  └──────────────┘ └──────────────┘ └──────────────┘ └──────────────┘  │  │
│  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  MY SCENARIOS                                              Filter ▼  Sort ▼ │
│  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │ □ │ Name                  │ Strategy  │ Status    │ Created   │ Actions│  │
│  ├───┼───────────────────────┼───────────┼───────────┼───────────┼────────┤  │
│  │ □ │ Q1 2026 Baseline      │ Balanced  │ ● Active  │ Jan 5     │ ⋮      │  │
│  │ □ │ High Utilization Test │ Aggressive│ ○ Draft   │ Jan 7     │ ⋮      │  │
│  │ □ │ PM Deferral Impact    │ Custom    │ ✓ Complete│ Jan 6     │ ⋮      │  │
│  │ □ │ Summer Peak Strategy  │ Seasonal  │ ○ Draft   │ Jan 8     │ ⋮      │  │
│  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  RECENT COMPARISONS                                                          │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────┐ ┌────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │ Baseline vs High Utilization       │ │ Conservative vs Cost Optimized │  │
│  │ Compared: 2 hours ago              │ │ Compared: Yesterday            │  │
│  │ Result: High Util +8% efficiency   │ │ Result: Cost saves $120k/yr    │  │
│  │ [View Comparison]                  │ │ [View Comparison]              │  │
│  └────────────────────────────────────┘ └────────────────────────────────┘  │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### 3.2 Quick Start Templates

| Template | Strategy | Description | Default Parameters |
|----------|----------|-------------|-------------------|
| **Aggressive Mileage** | Maximize utilization | Push trains to upper PM window | PM buffer: 5%, Tolerance: +10% |
| **Conservative Maintenance** | Maximize safety | Early PM scheduling | PM buffer: 20%, Tolerance: -5% |
| **Balanced Strategy** | Optimize all factors | Equal weight on metrics | PM buffer: 10%, Tolerance: 0% |
| **Cost Optimized** | Minimize cost within SLA | Defer PM to latest safe point | PM buffer: 3%, Tolerance: +8% |
| **Seasonal Peak** | Handle demand spikes | Higher utilization during peaks | Variable by month |
| **Fleet Rebalance** | Equalize mileage | Target uniform distribution | Balance target: 98% |

### 3.3 Scenario Status States

| Status | Icon | Color | Description |
|--------|------|-------|-------------|
| Draft | ○ | Gray #6B7280 | Scenario being configured |
| Running | ◐ | Blue #3B82F6 | Simulation in progress |
| Complete | ✓ | Green #22C55E | Results available |
| Active | ● | Purple #8B5CF6 | Applied to live planning |
| Archived | ◇ | Slate #94A3B8 | Historical reference |
| Error | ✕ | Red #EF4444 | Simulation failed |

---

## 4. Scenario Builder View

### 4.1 Layout Structure

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  ← Back to Manager        Scenario: "Q1 2026 High Utilization"      [Save]  │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  ┌───────────────────────────────────┐ ┌─────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │  STRATEGY PARAMETERS              │ │  SIMULATION PREVIEW             │  │
│  │                                   │ │                                 │  │
│  │  Base Strategy                    │ │  ┌─────────────────────────┐   │  │
│  │  [Aggressive ▼]                   │ │  │    PROJECTED OUTCOMES   │   │  │
│  │                                   │ │  │                         │   │  │
│  │  ─────────────────────────────    │ │  │  Fleet Util     +8.2%   │   │  │
│  │                                   │ │  │  PM Compliance  -2.1%   │   │  │
│  │  PM Window Buffer                 │ │  │  Cost Impact    -$45k   │   │  │
│  │  ├────────●─────────┤  5%         │ │  │  Risk Score     Medium  │   │  │
│  │  Early PM          Late PM        │ │  │                         │   │  │
│  │                                   │ │  └─────────────────────────┘   │  │
│  │  Mileage Tolerance                │ │                                 │  │
│  │  ├─────────────●────┤  +10%       │ │  ┌─────────────────────────┐   │  │
│  │  Underrun         Overrun         │ │  │  FLEET IMPACT (12 mo)   │   │  │
│  │                                   │ │  │                         │   │  │
│  │  Fleet Balance Target             │ │  │  ▓▓▓▓▓▓▓░░░ KS Fleet    │   │  │
│  │  ├────●─────────────┤  90%        │ │  │  ▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓░░ AL Fleet    │   │  │
│  │  Low                High          │ │  │                         │   │  │

│  │                                   │ │  │  47 in 90 days          │   │  │

│  │                                   │ │                                 │  │

### 4.2 Parameter Slider Design

Following Nielsen Norman Group's slider design guidelines:

**Slider Design Requirements:**
- **Thumb Size:** 32x32px with 44x44px touch target
- **Track Width:** Full container width minus 32px padding
- **Value Display:** Current value shown above slider thumb
- **Step Indicators:** Tick marks at 25% intervals
- **Real-Time Feedback:** Preview updates as slider moves
- **Reset Button:** Small "↺" icon to restore default value

```
PM Window Buffer
[↺]    5%
├─────┼─────┼─────●─────┼─────┤
0%   5%    10%   15%   20%   25%
Early PM ←───────────────→ Late PM
```

**Sources:**
- [NN/G Slider Design Rules of Thumb](https://www.nngroup.com/articles/gui-slider-controls/)
- [Smashing Magazine Perfect Slider UX](https://www.smashingmagazine.com/2017/07/designing-perfect-slider/)

### 4.3 Parameter Definitions

| Parameter | Range | Default | Impact |
|-----------|-------|---------|--------|
| **PM Window Buffer** | 0-25% | 10% | How early before PM threshold to schedule |
| **Mileage Tolerance** | -10% to +15% | 0% | Deviation from planned daily mileage |
| **Fleet Balance Target** | 70-100% | 90% | How evenly distributed mileage should be |
| **Man-Hours Available** | 50-100% | 90% | Resource capacity utilization |
| **Workshop Tracks** | 1-6 | 4 | Concurrent PM capacity |
| **Overtime Cap** | 0-30% | 15% | Maximum overtime percentage |

### 4.4 Real-Time Preview Panel

The preview panel updates within 500ms of parameter changes, showing:

1. **Projected Outcomes:** KPI delta from baseline
2. **Fleet Impact:** Progress bars by fleet type
3. **Risk Indicators:** Warnings for constraint violations
4. **Key Metrics:** 3-4 most relevant numbers

---

## 5. Scenario Comparison View

### 5.1 Side-by-Side Layout

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Compare Scenarios        [Side-by-Side] [Overlay] [Table]    [+ Add More]  │

│  │   Fleet Utilization: 78%       │ │   Fleet Utilization: 86% ▲8%   │    │
│  │   ▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓░░░░░░               │ │   ▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓░░░░               │    │
│  │                                 │ │                                 │    │
│  │   PM Compliance: 94%            │ │   PM Compliance: 92% ▼2%       │    │
│  │   ▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓░░░░░░              │ │   ▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓░░░░░░              │    │
│  │                                 │ │                                 │    │
│  │   Annual Cost: $2.4M            │ │   Annual Cost: $2.36M ▼$45k    │    │

├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  DETAILED COMPARISON                                                         │

│  │ Metric                    │ Baseline     │ High Util    │ Difference  │  │
│  ├───────────────────────────┼──────────────┼──────────────┼─────────────┤  │

│  │ Spare Parts Cost          │ $890k        │ $820k        │ -$70k 🟢    │  │
│  │ Labour Cost               │ $1.1M        │ $1.0M        │ -$100k 🟢   │  │
│  │ Overtime Hours            │ 240          │ 520          │ +280 🔴     │  │
│  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

```

### 5.2 Overlay View (Timeline Chart)

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Fleet Utilization Over Time                         [─ Baseline ─ High U]  │
│  100%│                                                                      │
│      │                    ●───●───●                                         │
│   90%│              ●───●         ●───●───●  ← High Utilization            │
│      │        ●───●                         ●                              │
│   80%│  ●───●                                                              │
│      │      ○───○───○───○───○───○───○───○───○───○  ← Baseline              │
│   70%│  ○                                                                   │
│      │                                                                      │
│   60%├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────│
│      Jan   Feb   Mar   Apr   May   Jun   Jul   Aug   Sep   Oct   Nov   Dec  │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### 5.3 Comparison Color Coding

| Indicator | Symbol | Meaning |
|-----------|--------|---------|
| 🟢 Green | Better than baseline | Favorable outcome |
| 🟡 Yellow | Within tolerance | Acceptable trade-off |
| 🔴 Red | Worse than baseline | Unfavorable outcome |
| ⬆ Arrow Up | Increase | Change direction |
| ⬇ Arrow Down | Decrease | Change direction |

### 5.4 Multi-Scenario Comparison (Up to 4)

When comparing 3-4 scenarios, use a horizontal table with:
- **Highlighted Winner:** Green background on best value per row
- **Color Gradient:** Heat map coloring from best to worst
- **Rank Indicator:** #1, #2, #3, #4 in corner of cells

**Sources:**
- [Agentnoon Scenario Comparisons](https://docs.agentnoon.com/scenarios/scenario-comparisons)
- [Visual Comparison Techniques for Before/After Analysis](https://dev3lop.com/visual-comparison-techniques-for-before-after-analysis/)

## 8. Gestalt Principles Applied

### 8.1 Proximity

- **Parameter Groups:** Related sliders grouped in labeled sections
- **Comparison Cards:** Side-by-side scenarios with clear separation
- **Action Buttons:** Primary actions grouped in toolbar

### 8.2 Similarity

- **Consistent Slider Design:** All parameter sliders have identical styling
- **Status Colors:** Same color system across all views
- **Card Layout:** Uniform card structure for scenarios

### 8.3 Enclosure

- **Panel Boundaries:** Clear card borders for each section
- **Comparison Frames:** Distinct frames for each scenario being compared
- **Result Groups:** Bordered sections for different metric categories

### 8.4 Continuity

- **Timeline Flow:** Left-to-right progression in projections
- **Parameter Order:** Logical top-to-bottom priority ordering
- **Visual Alignment:** Grid-based layout for comparison tables

### 8.5 Figure-Ground

- **Active Scenario:** Elevated shadow for selected scenario
- **Hover States:** Subtle elevation on interactive elements
- **Comparison Highlight:** Background tint on "winner" column

### 8.6 Common Fate

- **Linked Sliders:** Connected parameters animate together
- **Comparison Sync:** Scroll position synced across compared scenarios
- **Selection Propagation:** Multi-select actions apply uniformly

---

## 9. Interaction Patterns

### 9.1 Create New Scenario Flow

```
[Start] → Select Template → Adjust Parameters → Preview Results → [Save/Run]
          (or Blank)       (real-time update)   (validate)
```

### 9.2 Compare Scenarios Flow

```
[Select 2-4 scenarios] → Choose View Mode → Analyze Differences → [Export/Apply]
                         (Side/Overlay/Table)
```

### 9.3 Parameter Adjustment Interaction

1. **Drag slider thumb** → Value updates in real-time
2. **Click track** → Thumb jumps to click position
3. **Keyboard arrows** → Increment/decrement by step
4. **Type value** → Direct input in value field
5. **Reset button** → Return to default value

### 9.4 Real-Time Feedback Timing

| Action | Response Time | Feedback Type |
|--------|---------------|---------------|
| Slider drag | < 100ms | Value label update |
| Slider release | < 500ms | Preview panel update |
| Run simulation | < 3s | Progress indicator |
| Load comparison | < 1s | Side-by-side render |

---

## 10. Responsive Design

### 10.1 Desktop (≥1200px)

- Full side-by-side comparison (2-4 scenarios)
- Multi-column parameter layout
- Expanded charts with full legends

### 10.2 Tablet (768-1199px)

- Stacked comparison (scroll between scenarios)
- Single-column parameter layout
- Condensed chart legends

### 10.3 Mobile (<768px)

- **Limited Functionality:** View results only, no scenario building
- Swipe between scenarios
- Collapsed parameter summary
- Simplified chart views

**Rationale:** Scenario building requires precise parameter adjustment that is difficult on small screens. Mobile users can review and compare existing scenarios but should use desktop/tablet for creation.

---

## 11. Accessibility Requirements

### 11.1 Keyboard Navigation

| Key | Action |
|-----|--------|
| Tab | Move between interactive elements |
| Arrow Left/Right | Adjust slider value |
| Enter | Confirm selection / Run simulation |
| Escape | Cancel / Close modal |
| Space | Toggle checkbox / Expand panel |

### 11.2 Screen Reader Support

- **ARIA Labels:** All sliders have descriptive labels
- **Live Regions:** Result changes announced
- **Value Announcements:** Slider values read on change

### 11.3 Color Contrast

- All text meets WCAG AA (4.5:1 minimum)
- Status indicators have icon+text (not color alone)
- Chart series differentiated by pattern + color

---

## 12. Unknown Unknowns Discovered

Through research and analysis, the following "unknown unknowns" were identified:

### 12.1 Simulation Complexity

1. **Multi-year compounding:** Scenarios spanning 12 years have compounding effects that are hard to visualize
2. **Interdependent parameters:** Changing one slider may require automatic adjustment of others
3. **Stochastic vs deterministic:** Users may expect different randomness handling
4. **Partial scenario runs:** What if simulation is stopped mid-way?

### 12.2 User Collaboration

5. **Concurrent editing:** What if two planners modify the same scenario?
6. **Version history:** How to track changes to scenario parameters over time?
7. **Approval workflow:** Should applying a scenario to live planning require approval?
8. **Template ownership:** Who can create/modify shared templates?

### 12.3 Data Quality

9. **Input validation:** How to handle impossible parameter combinations?
10. **Data freshness:** How recent must mileage data be for accurate simulation?
11. **External factors:** How to model unplanned events (breakdowns, weather)?
12. **Seasonal patterns:** Should scenarios automatically adjust for known peaks?

### 12.4 Performance

13. **Simulation time:** Long-horizon scenarios may take minutes - how to handle UX?
14. **Cache invalidation:** When should cached results be recalculated?
15. **Partial results:** Can users see preliminary results while simulation runs?
16. **Resource limits:** How many concurrent simulations per user?

### 12.5 Reporting Integration

17. **Audit trail:** Must scenario decisions be logged for regulatory compliance?
18. **Report embedding:** Can scenario results be embedded in external reports?
19. **Data export format:** What formats do downstream systems require?
20. **Historical comparison:** How long to retain old scenario results?

---

   210→│                                                                             │

   221→## 5. Export Formats

   228→| **Excel (.xlsx)** | Data analysis, manipulation | Formulas, pivot tables, charts |

   272→### 5.3 Excel Export Features

   283→## 6. Scheduled Reports

   294→│  │✓ │ Daily Fleet Summary   │ Daily 8AM │ Tomorrow   │ 3         │ ⋮     │  │
   295→│  │✓ │ Weekly PM Status      │ Mon 9AM   │ Jan 13     │ 5         │ ⋮     │  │
   296→│  │✓ │ Monthly Budget Report │ 1st 10AM  │ Feb 1      │ 8         │ ⋮     │  │

   356→
   357→## 7. Custom Report Builder
   358→
   359→### 7.1 Builder Interface Layout
   360→
   361→```
   362→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   363→│  Report Builder: "Custom Fleet Analysis"                    [Save] [Preview]│
   364→├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
   365→│  ┌─────────────────┐ ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
   366→│  │  DATA SOURCES   │ │  REPORT CANVAS                                    │  │
   367→│  │                 │ │                                                   │  │
   368→│  │  📊 Trains      │ │  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │  │
   369→│  │    • Train ID   │ │  │  [Header Component]                          │ │  │
   370→│  │    • Fleet Type │ │  │  Custom Fleet Analysis Report               │ │  │
   371→│  │    • Mileage    │ │  │  Period: {start_date} - {end_date}          │ │  │
   372→│  │    • Status     │ │  └─────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │  │
   373→│  │                 │ │                                                   │  │
   374→│  │  📈 Mileage     │ │  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │  │
   375→│  │    • Daily      │ │  │  [KPI Cards Row]                             │ │  │
   376→│  │    • Cumulative │ │  │  ┌───────┐ ┌───────┐ ┌───────┐ ┌───────┐   │ │  │
   377→│  │    • Deviation  │ │  │  │Total  │ │Avg    │ │PM Due │ │Balance│   │ │  │
   378→│  │                 │ │  │  │Trains │ │Mileage│ │Soon   │ │Score  │   │ │  │
   379→│  │  🔧 Maintenance │ │  │  │{count}│ │{avg}  │ │{count}│ │{score}│   │ │  │
   380→│  │    • PM Events  │ │  │  └───────┘ └───────┘ └───────┘ └───────┘   │ │  │
   381→│  │    • Compliance │ │  └─────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │  │
   382→│  │    • Cost       │ │                                                   │  │
   383→│  │                 │ │  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │  │
   384→│  ├─────────────────┤ │  │  [Data Table Component]                      │ │  │
   385→│  │  COMPONENTS     │ │  │  Train│Fleet│Mileage│PM Due│Status         │ │  │
   386→│  │                 │ │  │  ─────────────────────────────              │ │  │
   387→│  │  📝 Text        │ │  │  {data rows...}                              │ │  │
   388→│  │  📊 Table       │ │  └─────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │  │
   389→│  │  📈 Chart       │ │                                                   │  │
   390→│  │  🔢 KPI Card    │ │  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │  │
   391→│  │  📋 Summary     │ │  │  [Chart Component]                           │ │  │
   392→│  │  📄 Page Break  │ │  │  [Mileage Trend by Fleet]                   │ │  │
   393→│  │  🖼️ Image       │ │  └─────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │  │
   394→│  └─────────────────┘ └───────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
   395→├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
   396→│  COMPONENT PROPERTIES                                                        │
   397→│  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
   398→│  │  Selected: [Data Table]    Columns: [Configure...]    Filter: [Add...] │  │

   401→└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

   403→
   404→### 7.2 Builder Components
   405→
   406→| Component | Purpose | Configuration |
   407→|-----------|---------|---------------|
   408→| **Header** | Report title, logo, date | Title text, logo toggle, date format |
   409→| **Text** | Static text, descriptions | Rich text editor |
   410→| **KPI Card** | Single metric display | Data field, format, comparison |
   411→| **Table** | Data rows and columns | Columns, sort, filter, grouping |
   412→| **Chart** | Visual data representation | Type, series, axes, legend |
   413→| **Summary** | Aggregated statistics | Metrics, calculations |
   414→| **Page Break** | PDF pagination | Before/after page break |
   415→| **Image** | Logos, diagrams | Upload, URL, sizing |
   416→
   417→### 7.3 Data Binding
   418→
   419→Components can bind to data using:
   420→- **Field References:** `{train.mileage}`, `{maintenance.compliance}`
   421→- **Expressions:** `{SUM(mileage.daily)}`, `{AVG(cost.labor)}`
   422→- **Conditions:** `{IF(compliance > 90, "✓", "✗")}`
   423→- **Formatting:** `{FORMAT(date, "MMMM YYYY")}`
   424→
   425→---
   426→
   427→## 8. Report History
   428→
   429→### 8.1 History List Layout
   430→
   431→```
   432→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   433→│  Report History                                              [🔍 Search]     │
   434→├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
   435→│  Filter: [All Reports ▼]  Date: [Last 30 Days ▼]  Status: [All ▼]          │
   436→├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
   437→│  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
   438→│  │ Report Name           │ Generated   │ By       │ Status  │ Actions   │  │
   439→│  ├───────────────────────┼─────────────┼──────────┼─────────┼───────────┤  │
   440→│  │ PM Compliance Report  │ Today 14:32 │ J. Doe   │ ✓ Ready │ 📥⋮       │  │
   441→│  │ Fleet Mileage Status  │ Today 08:00 │ Scheduled│ ✓ Ready │ 📥⋮       │  │
   442→│  │ Budget vs Actual      │ Yesterday   │ System   │ ✓ Ready │ 📥⋮       │  │
   443→│  │ Custom Fleet Analysis │ Jan 5       │ J. Smith │ ⚠ Warning│ 📥⋮       │  │
   444→│  │ Weekly PM Status      │ Jan 6       │ Scheduled│ ✓ Ready │ 📥⋮       │  │
   445→│  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
   446→│                                                                             │
   447→│  Showing 5 of 127 reports    [1] [2] [3] ... [26] [→]                      │
   448→└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
   449→```
   450→
   451→### 8.2 Report Status
   452→
   453→| Status | Icon | Description |
   454→|--------|------|-------------|
   455→| Ready | ✓ | Report generated successfully |
   456→| Processing | ◐ | Report generation in progress |
   457→| Warning | ⚠ | Generated with warnings |
   458→| Error | ✗ | Generation failed |
   459→| Expired | ◇ | Report past retention date |
   460→
   461→### 8.3 Retention Policy
   462→
   463→- **Standard Reports:** 90 days
   464→- **Compliance Reports:** 7 years (regulatory requirement per FRA)
   465→- **Custom Reports:** 30 days (configurable)
   466→- **Archive:** Manual archive for permanent storage
   467→
   468→---
   469→
   470→## 9. Gestalt Principles Applied
   471→
   472→### 9.1 Proximity
   473→
   474→- **Report Cards:** Related info grouped (name, description, formats)
   475→- **Parameters:** Grouped by category (period, fleet, details)
   476→- **Schedule Fields:** Frequency options near time options
   477→
   478→### 9.2 Similarity
   479→
   480→- **Report Icons:** Consistent category-specific icons
   481→- **Status Indicators:** Same color/icon system across views
   482→- **Action Buttons:** Uniform button styling
   483→
   484→### 9.3 Enclosure
   485→
   486→- **Report Cards:** Clear boundaries for each report
   487→- **Modal Windows:** Defined generation/schedule containers
   488→- **Builder Canvas:** Distinct component areas
   489→
   490→### 9.4 Continuity
   491→
   492→- **Generation Flow:** Left-to-right parameter progression
   493→- **Schedule Timeline:** Chronological list ordering
   494→- **Builder Canvas:** Top-to-bottom report structure
   495→
   496→### 9.5 Figure-Ground
   497→
   498→- **Active Report:** Elevated shadow on selected
   499→- **Drag Components:** Lifted appearance during drag
   500→- **Modals:** Dimmed background for focus
   501→
   502→---
   503→
   504→## 10. Accessibility Requirements
   505→
   506→### 10.1 Keyboard Navigation
   507→
   508→| Key | Action |
   509→|-----|--------|
   510→| Tab | Navigate between fields/buttons |
   511→| Enter | Confirm selection / Generate |
   512→| Escape | Close modal / Cancel |
   513→| Arrow Keys | Navigate list items |
   514→| Space | Toggle checkboxes |
   515→
   516→### 10.2 Screen Reader Support
   517→
   518→- **ARIA Labels:** All buttons and inputs labeled
   519→- **Live Regions:** Progress updates announced
   520→- **Table Headers:** Proper scope attributes
   521→- **Status Changes:** Role="status" for notifications
   522→
   523→### 10.3 Color Contrast
   524→
   525→- All text meets WCAG AA (4.5:1 minimum)
   526→- Status indicators use icon + text (not color alone)
   527→- Focus states clearly visible
   528→
   529→---
   530→
   531→## 11. Unknown Unknowns Discovered
   532→
   533→### 11.1 Report Content
   534→
   535→1. **Multi-language reports:** International stakeholders may need localized reports
   536→2. **Data currency:** Report should show data freshness/last sync time
   537→3. **Drill-down links:** PDF links to web app for live data
   538→4. **Digital signatures:** Compliance reports may need e-signatures
   539→5. **Watermarks:** Draft vs Final report distinction
   540→
   541→### 11.2 Distribution
   542→
   543→6. **Email size limits:** Large PDF attachments may be blocked
   544→7. **External recipients:** Security for sharing outside organization
   545→8. **Notification preferences:** Some users may prefer Slack/Teams
   546→9. **Time zone handling:** Scheduled reports across time zones
   547→10. **Holiday calendars:** Skip scheduled reports on holidays?
   548→
   549→### 11.3 Technical
   550→
   551→11. **Report caching:** How long to cache large reports?
   552→12. **Concurrent generation:** Queue management for multiple users
   553→13. **Partial failures:** Handling incomplete data sources
   554→14. **Version compatibility:** Excel format compatibility
   555→15. **Mobile report viewing:** Responsive PDF viewing
   556→
   557→### 11.4 Compliance
   558→
   559→16. **Audit trail:** Who accessed which reports and when
   560→17. **Data masking:** PII handling in shared reports
   561→18. **Retention compliance:** Different retention per report type
   562→19. **Report amendments:** How to handle corrections to published reports
   563→20. **Chain of custody:** For regulatory submissions
   564→
   565→---
   566→
   567→## 12. Standard Report Templates
   568→
   569→### 12.1 PM Compliance Report

   571→**Sections:**

   580→### 12.2 Fleet Mileage Status Report

   582→**Sections:**
   583→1. Summary Statistics
   584→2. Mileage Distribution Chart
   585→3. Train-by-Train Status Table
   586→4. PM Window Status
   587→5. Deviation Alerts
   588→6. Balance Analysis
   589→
   590→### 12.3 Budget vs Actual Report
   591→
   592→**Sections:**
   593→1. Cost Summary
   594→2. Variance Analysis (Waterfall)
   595→3. Category Breakdown
   596→4. Fleet Comparison
   597→5. Trend Analysis
   598→6. Forecast to Year-End
   599→
   600→### 12.4 Executive Summary Report
   601→
   602→**Sections:**
   603→1. KPI Dashboard (1 page)
   604→2. Key Highlights
   605→3. Risks and Issues
   606→4. Action Items
   607→5. 12-Month Outlook
   608→
   609→---
   610→
   611→## 13. TypeScript Types Reference
   612→
   613→See: `src/mileage/types/report-ui.ts`

   616→- `ReportCategory`, `ReportTemplate`, `ReportFormat`
   617→- `ReportParameter`, `ParameterType`, `ParameterValidation`

   622→- `ReportBuilderState`, `ReportLibraryState`, `ScheduleListState`

# Report Generation Templates Specification

**Document ID:** MILEAGE-UX-008
**Version:** 1.0
**Created:** 2026-01-08
**RFQ Reference:** Sections 5, 6, 8

---

## 1. Overview

### 1.1 Purpose

This specification defines the user experience for the **Report Generation** module, enabling users to generate, schedule, customize, and export reports for maintenance compliance, mileage tracking, cost analysis, and operational performance across the 128-train fleet over the 12-year contract period.

### 1.2 Target Users

| Role | Primary Tasks | Report Focus |
|------|---------------|--------------|
| Maintenance Manager | PM compliance, backlog reports | Schedule adherence |
| Operations Manager | Daily/weekly summaries | Utilization |
| Finance Controller | Cost reports, variance analysis | Budget compliance |
| Contract Manager | SLA compliance reports | KPI tracking |
| Executive | Executive summaries | High-level metrics |
| Auditor | Compliance documentation | Regulatory reports |

### 1.3 RFQ Requirements Addressed

- **Section 5:** "System generates maintenance reports"
- **Section 5:** "Monthly/yearly cost compliance reports"
- **Section 6:** "Mileage status reports for all trains"
- **Section 8:** "Man-hour projection reports"

### 1.4 Competitor Research Insights

| Competitor | Key Feature | Applicable Insight |
|------------|-------------|-------------------|
| **Simply Fleet** | Free Excel/PDF templates | Multi-format export, budget templates |
| **ClickUp** | Excel compatibility | Cross-platform export |
| **Microsoft Report Builder** | Drag-and-drop design | Visual report designer |
| **Telerik Reporting** | Scheduled automation | Pixel-perfect + scheduling |
| **SafetyCulture** | Compliance checklists | Transportation-specific templates |

**Sources:**
- [Simply Fleet Templates](https://www.simplyfleet.app/free-template-checklists-fleet)
- [Microsoft Report Builder](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/sql/reporting-services/report-builder/report-design-view-report-builder)
- [FRA Compliance Requirements](https://railroads.dot.gov/guidance)

## 2. Information Architecture

### 2.1 Screen Hierarchy

```
Reports
├── Standard Reports (Default)
│   ├── Report Library
│   ├── Report Preview
│   ├── Generate Report
│   └── Export Options
├── Scheduled Reports
│   ├── Schedule List
│   ├── Create Schedule
│   ├── Schedule History
│   └── Distribution Settings
├── Custom Reports
│   ├── Report Builder
│   ├── Template Library
│   ├── My Templates
│   └── Share Templates
└── Report History
    ├── Generated Reports
    ├── Archive
    └── Search Reports
```

### 2.2 Navigation Pattern

- **Tabs:** Standard | Scheduled | Custom | History
- **Breadcrumb:** Reports > [Category] > [Report Name]
- **Context Actions:** Generate, Schedule, Export, Share

## 3. Standard Report Library

### 3.1 Report Categories

| Category | Reports | Frequency |
|----------|---------|-----------|
| **Maintenance** | PM Compliance, Backlog Status, Overdue Analysis, Maintenance Summary | Daily/Weekly/Monthly |
| **Mileage** | Fleet Mileage Status, Deviation Report, Balance Analysis, Trend Report | Daily/Weekly |
| **Cost** | Budget vs Actual, Variance Analysis, Cost Breakdown, Forecast Report | Monthly/Quarterly |
| **Operations** | Run Assignment Summary, Utilization Report, Fleet Availability | Daily/Weekly |
| **Compliance** | SLA Compliance, Regulatory Report, Audit Trail | Monthly/Quarterly |
| **Executive** | Executive Summary, KPI Dashboard, 12-Month Outlook | Monthly |

|-----------|---------|---------|

| **On-Demand** | Triggered by event | On PM completion |
| **Custom** | Cron expression | Complex schedules |

### 7.2 Builder Components

| Component | Purpose | Configuration |
|-----------|---------|---------------|
| **Header** | Report title, logo, date | Title text, logo toggle, date format |
| **Text** | Static text, descriptions | Rich text editor |
| **KPI Card** | Single metric display | Data field, format, comparison |
| **Table** | Data rows and columns | Columns, sort, filter, grouping |
| **Chart** | Visual data representation | Type, series, axes, legend |
| **Summary** | Aggregated statistics | Metrics, calculations |
| **Page Break** | PDF pagination | Before/after page break |
| **Image** | Logos, diagrams | Upload, URL, sizing |

### 7.3 Data Binding

Components can bind to data using:
- **Field References:** `{train.mileage}`, `{maintenance.compliance}`
- **Expressions:** `{SUM(mileage.daily)}`, `{AVG(cost.labor)}`
- **Conditions:** `{IF(compliance > 90, "✓", "✗")}`
- **Formatting:** `{FORMAT(date, "MMMM YYYY")}`

|--------|------|-------------|

| Processing | ◐ | Report generation in progress |
| Warning | ⚠ | Generated with warnings |
| Error | ✗ | Generation failed |
| Expired | ◇ | Report past retention date |

### 8.3 Retention Policy

- **Standard Reports:** 90 days
- **Compliance Reports:** 7 years (regulatory requirement per FRA)
- **Custom Reports:** 30 days (configurable)
- **Archive:** Manual archive for permanent storage

## 9. Gestalt Principles Applied

### 9.1 Proximity

- **Report Cards:** Related info grouped (name, description, formats)
- **Parameters:** Grouped by category (period, fleet, details)
- **Schedule Fields:** Frequency options near time options

### 9.2 Similarity

- **Report Icons:** Consistent category-specific icons
- **Status Indicators:** Same color/icon system across views
- **Action Buttons:** Uniform button styling

### 9.3 Enclosure

- **Report Cards:** Clear boundaries for each report
- **Modal Windows:** Defined generation/schedule containers
- **Builder Canvas:** Distinct component areas

### 9.4 Continuity

- **Generation Flow:** Left-to-right parameter progression
- **Schedule Timeline:** Chronological list ordering
- **Builder Canvas:** Top-to-bottom report structure

### 9.5 Figure-Ground

- **Active Report:** Elevated shadow on selected
- **Drag Components:** Lifted appearance during drag
- **Modals:** Dimmed background for focus

---

## 10. Accessibility Requirements

### 10.1 Keyboard Navigation

| Key | Action |
|-----|--------|
| Tab | Navigate between fields/buttons |
| Enter | Confirm selection / Generate |
| Escape | Close modal / Cancel |
| Arrow Keys | Navigate list items |
| Space | Toggle checkboxes |

### 10.2 Screen Reader Support

- **ARIA Labels:** All buttons and inputs labeled
- **Live Regions:** Progress updates announced
- **Table Headers:** Proper scope attributes
- **Status Changes:** Role="status" for notifications

### 10.3 Color Contrast

- All text meets WCAG AA (4.5:1 minimum)
- Status indicators use icon + text (not color alone)
- Focus states clearly visible

---

## 11. Unknown Unknowns Discovered

### 11.1 Report Content

1. **Multi-language reports:** International stakeholders may need localized reports
2. **Data currency:** Report should show data freshness/last sync time
3. **Drill-down links:** PDF links to web app for live data
4. **Digital signatures:** Compliance reports may need e-signatures
5. **Watermarks:** Draft vs Final report distinction

### 11.2 Distribution

6. **Email size limits:** Large PDF attachments may be blocked
7. **External recipients:** Security for sharing outside organization
8. **Notification preferences:** Some users may prefer Slack/Teams
9. **Time zone handling:** Scheduled reports across time zones
10. **Holiday calendars:** Skip scheduled reports on holidays?

### 11.3 Technical

11. **Report caching:** How long to cache large reports?
12. **Concurrent generation:** Queue management for multiple users
13. **Partial failures:** Handling incomplete data sources
14. **Version compatibility:** Excel format compatibility
15. **Mobile report viewing:** Responsive PDF viewing

### 11.4 Compliance

16. **Audit trail:** Who accessed which reports and when
17. **Data masking:** PII handling in shared reports
18. **Retention compliance:** Different retention per report type
19. **Report amendments:** How to handle corrections to published reports
20. **Chain of custody:** For regulatory submissions

1→# Alert & Notification System UX Specification
     2→
     3→## MILEAGE-UX-009 - Rolling Stock Mileage Management
     4→
     5→**Version:** 1.0
     6→**Last Updated:** 2026-01-08
     7→**Status:** Complete
     8→
     9→---
    10→
    11→## 1. Overview
    12→
    13→### Purpose
    14→Design a comprehensive alert and notification system for the Rolling Stock Mileage Management platform that keeps stakeholders informed about critical events while preventing notification fatigue.
    15→
    16→### Scope
    17→- 128 trains (49 Kinki Sharyo + 79 Alstom)
    18→- 12-year contract period
    19→- Multiple user roles (Operations, Maintenance, Management, Executive)
    20→- RFQ Section 5 compliance: "Email notification for underrun and overrun mileage"
    21→
    22→### Design Principles
    23→1. **Assist, Don't Impede**: Notifications help users complete tasks
    24→2. **Context Matters**: Provide actionable information
    25→3. **User Control**: Prevent fatigue with customization
    26→4. **Tiered Urgency**: Not all alerts warrant same attention
    27→5. **Clear Escalation**: Automatic escalation for unacknowledged critical alerts
    28→
    29→### Sources Referenced
    30→- [Smashing Magazine: Design Guidelines For Better Notifications UX](https://www.smashingmagazine.com/2025/07/design-guidelines-better-notifications-ux/)
    31→- [Toptal: Comprehensive Guide to Notification Design](https://www.toptal.com/designers/ux/notification-design)
    32→- [MagicBell: Notification System Design](https://www.magicbell.com/blog/notification-system-design)
    33→- [Carbon Design System: Notification Pattern](https://carbondesignsystem.com/patterns/notification-pattern/)
    34→- [Fleetio: Fleet Management Notifications](https://www.fleetio.com/features/fleet-management-notifications)
    35→- [Pitstop: Alert Management Workflows](https://pitstopconnect.com/2024/03/05/5-steps-effective-alert-management-workflows-fleet-operations/)
    36→
    37→---
    38→
    39→## 2. Alert Categories & Priorities
    40→
    41→### 2.1 Priority Levels
    42→
    43→| Priority | Label | Color | Response Time | Auto-Escalate |
    44→|----------|-------|-------|---------------|---------------|
    45→| P1 | Critical | #DC2626 (Red) | Immediate | 15 min |
    46→| P2 | Warning | #F59E0B (Amber) | 4 hours | 24 hours |
    47→| P3 | Info | #3B82F6 (Blue) | 24 hours | Never |
    48→| P4 | Success | #22C55E (Green) | N/A | Never |
    49→
    50→### 2.2 Alert Categories
    51→
    52→#### Maintenance Alerts
    53→| Alert Type | Priority | Trigger Condition | Audience |
    54→|------------|----------|-------------------|----------|
    55→| PM Overdue | P1 | Train past PM threshold | Maintenance, Ops |
    56→| PM Critical | P1 | Train >= 95% of PM threshold | Maintenance |
    57→| PM Approaching | P2 | Train >= 80% of PM threshold | Maintenance |
    58→| PM Scheduled | P3 | PM scheduled within 7 days | Maintenance, Ops |
    59→| PM Completed | P4 | PM successfully completed | Maintenance, Ops |
    60→| Inspection Failed | P1 | Vehicle inspection item failed | Maintenance |
    61→| Fault Code Triggered | P2 | OBD fault code detected | Maintenance |
    62→
    63→#### Mileage Alerts (RFQ Section 5)
    64→| Alert Type | Priority | Trigger Condition | Audience |
    65→|------------|----------|-------------------|----------|
    66→| Mileage Overrun | P2 | Train >110% of target mileage | Operations, Management |
    67→| Mileage Underrun | P2 | Train <90% of target mileage | Operations, Management |
    68→| Fleet Imbalance | P2 | Fleet deviation >15% | Operations |
    69→| Mileage Forecast Alert | P3 | Projected PM date conflict | Maintenance |
    70→| Daily Mileage Missing | P2 | No mileage recorded for 24+ hours | Operations |
    71→
    72→#### Assignment Alerts
    73→| Alert Type | Priority | Trigger Condition | Audience |
    74→|------------|----------|-------------------|----------|
    75→| No Suitable Train | P1 | Cannot assign any train to route | Operations |
    76→| Assignment Conflict | P2 | Double-booking detected | Operations |
    77→| Schedule Not Published | P2 | Schedule not published by cutoff | Operations |
    78→| Low Recommendation Score | P3 | Best score <50% | Operations |
    79→| Assignment Changed | P3 | External system modified assignment | Operations |
    80→
    81→#### Resource Alerts
    82→| Alert Type | Priority | Trigger Condition | Audience |
    83→|------------|----------|-------------------|----------|
    84→| Man-Hour Shortage | P1 | Gap < -1000 man-hours | Management |
    85→| Track Overbooked | P1 | Track utilization >100% | Maintenance |
    86→| Resource Near Capacity | P2 | Utilization >90% | Maintenance |
    87→| Overtime Required | P3 | Gap requires overtime | Management |
    88→
    89→#### Cost Alerts
    90→| Alert Type | Priority | Trigger Condition | Audience |
    91→|------------|----------|-------------------|----------|
    92→| Budget Exceeded | P1 | Category >110% of budget | Management, Finance |
    93→| Budget Warning | P2 | Category >95% of budget | Management |
    94→| Cost Variance | P3 | Monthly variance >5% | Management |
    95→
    96→#### System Alerts
    97→| Alert Type | Priority | Trigger Condition | Audience |
    98→|------------|----------|-------------------|----------|
    99→| Integration Failure | P1 | Maximo sync failed | IT, Admin |
   100→| Data Sync Delayed | P2 | Sync >30 minutes late | IT |
   101→| Report Generation Failed | P2 | Scheduled report failed | Admin |
   102→| Session Expiring | P3 | User session ending soon | All |
   103→
   104→---
   105→
   106→## 3. Notification Types
   107→
   108→### 3.1 Toast Notifications (Transient)
   109→
   110→**Use For:** Real-time feedback, confirmations, non-critical updates
   111→
   112→```
   113→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   114→│ ✓  Assignment saved successfully                  × │
   115→│    Train KS-001 assigned to Route R-BLUE-01         │
   116→│                                          [Undo]     │
   117→└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
   118→```
   119→
   120→**Specifications:**
   121→- Position: Top-right corner
   122→- Duration: 5 seconds (auto-dismiss)
   123→- Max visible: 3 stacked
   124→- Animation: Slide in from right
   125→- Action: Single CTA (e.g., Undo, View)
   126→
   127→### 3.2 Inline Notifications (Contextual)
   128→
   129→**Use For:** Page-specific alerts, form validation, workflow status
   130→
   131→```
   132→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   133→│ ⚠ Warning                                           │
   134→│ 3 trains are approaching PM threshold this week.    │
   135→│ Review the PM schedule to ensure timely service.    │
   136→│                              [View Schedule]        │
   137→└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
   138→```
   139→
   140→**Specifications:**
   141→- Position: Top of relevant content area
   142→- Dismissible: Yes (with × button)
   143→- Persistence: Until dismissed or condition resolved
   144→- Actions: Up to 2 CTAs
   145→
   146→### 3.3 Actionable Notifications (Require Response)
   147→
   148→**Use For:** Critical alerts requiring user decision
   149→
   150→```
   151→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   152→│ ⛔ Critical: PM Overdue                             │
   153→│                                                     │
   154→│ Train KS-045 has exceeded PM2 threshold by 1,234    │
   155→│ km. Immediate action required.                      │
   156→│                                                     │
   157→│ Current Mileage: 46,234 km                         │
   158→│ PM2 Threshold: 45,000 km                           │
   159→│ Overdue By: 1,234 km (2.7%)                        │
   160→│                                                     │
   161→│ [Schedule PM Now]  [Defer with Approval]  [Dismiss] │
   162→└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
   163→```
   164→
   165→**Specifications:**
   166→- Position: Top of page or modal
   167→- Dismissible: Yes (but logged)
   168→- Persistence: Until action taken
   169→- Actions: Up to 3 CTAs
   170→- Audit: All dismissals logged
   171→
   172→### 3.4 Modal Notifications (Blocking)
   173→
   174→**Use For:** Critical decisions, confirmations with consequences
   175→
   176→```
   177→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   178→│                                                   × │
   179→│         ⚠ Confirm Schedule Override                 │
   180→│                                                     │
   181→│  You are about to override the AI recommendation    │
   182→│  for Route R-RED-01.                               │
   183→│                                                     │
   184→│  AI Suggested: KS-012 (Score: 87%)                 │
   185→│  Your Selection: KS-045 (Score: 62%)               │
   186→│                                                     │
   187→│  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
   188→│  │ Reason for override (required):                │ │
   189→│  │ ┌───────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ │
   190→│  │ │ Customer requested specific train          │ │ │
   191→│  │ └───────────────────────────────────────────┘ │ │
   192→│  └───────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
   193→│                                                     │
   194→│              [Cancel]  [Confirm Override]           │
   195→└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
   196→```
   197→
   198→---

   281→## 5. Delivery Channels
   282→
   283→### 5.1 Channel Matrix
   284→
   285→| Alert Type | In-App | Push | Email | SMS |
   286→|------------|--------|------|-------|-----|
   287→| PM Overdue (P1) | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
   288→| PM Critical (P1) | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | Optional |
   289→| PM Approaching (P2) | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | - |
   290→| Mileage Overrun (P2) | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | - |
   291→| Mileage Underrun (P2) | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | - |
   292→| Assignment Conflict (P2) | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | - |
   293→| Budget Warning (P2) | ✓ | - | ✓ | - |
   294→| PM Completed (P4) | ✓ | - | Digest | - |
   295→| Info Notifications (P3) | ✓ | - | Digest | - |
   296→
   297→### 5.2 Push Notification Format
   298→
   299→```
   300→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   301→│ 🚂 Rolling Stock Mileage                    │
   302→│                                             │
   303→│ PM Overdue: KS-045                         │
   304→│ Train exceeded PM2 threshold by 1,234 km.  │
   305→│ Tap to take action.                        │
   306→│                                   12:34 PM │
   307→└─────────────────────────────────────────────┘
   308→```
   309→
   310→**Character Limits:**
   311→- Title: 50 characters
   312→- Body: 150 characters
   313→- Include train ID and key metric
   314→
   315→### 5.3 Email Notification Template
   316→
   317→```

   323→
   324→CRITICAL ALERT: PM Overdue
   325→
   326→Train KS-045 has exceeded the PM2 maintenance threshold.

   333→│ Overdue By:      1,234 km (2.7%)          │

   336→
   337→RECOMMENDED ACTION:
   338→Schedule PM2 maintenance immediately to ensure

   341→[Schedule PM Now]  [View Train Details]
   342→
   343→─────────────────────────────────────────────
   344→
   345→This alert was sent to: maintenance@railoperator.com

   347→
   348→Manage notification preferences: [Settings]
   349→Unsubscribe from this alert type: [Unsubscribe]
   350→```

   384→---
   385→
   386→## 6. Escalation Workflow
   387→
   388→### 6.1 Escalation Tiers
   389→
   390→```
   391→TIER 1 (Immediate)
   392→├── Notification to: Assigned user/team
   393→├── Channels: In-app + Push + Email
   394→├── Wait time: 15 minutes
   395→└── If not acknowledged → TIER 2
   396→
   397→TIER 2 (Supervisor)
   398→├── Notification to: Team supervisor
   399→├── Channels: In-app + Push + Email + SMS
   400→├── Wait time: 30 minutes
   401→└── If not acknowledged → TIER 3
   402→
   403→TIER 3 (Management)
   404→├── Notification to: Department manager
   405→├── Channels: All channels
   406→├── Wait time: 1 hour
   407→└── If not acknowledged → TIER 4
   408→
   409→TIER 4 (Executive)
   410→├── Notification to: Operations director
   411→├── Channels: All channels + Phone call
   412→└── Tracked until resolved
   413→```
   414→
   415→### 6.2 Escalation Matrix
   416→
   417→| Alert Type | Tier 1 | Tier 2 | Tier 3 | Tier 4 |
   418→|------------|--------|--------|--------|--------|
   419→| PM Overdue | Maintenance Tech | Maintenance Sup | Maintenance Mgr | Ops Director |
   420→| Track Overbooked | Scheduler | Planning Sup | Planning Mgr | Ops Director |
   421→| No Suitable Train | Dispatcher | Ops Supervisor | Ops Manager | Ops Director |
   422→| Budget Exceeded | Accountant | Finance Sup | Finance Mgr | CFO |
   423→| Integration Failure | IT Support | IT Lead | IT Manager | CTO |
   424→
   425→### 6.3 Acknowledgment Actions
   426→
   427→| Action | Effect |
   428→|--------|--------|
   429→| Acknowledge | Stops escalation, marks as "In Progress" |
   430→| Defer | Reschedules alert for later, requires reason |
   431→| Resolve | Closes alert, updates status |
   432→| Escalate | Manually escalates to next tier |
   433→| Reassign | Assigns to different user/team |
   434→
   435→---
   436→
