## 10. Progressive Web App (PWA)

### 10.1 Manifest Configuration

```json
{
  "name": "Rolling Stock Mileage Management",
  "short_name": "RSMM",
  "description": "Fleet mileage management for 128 trains",
  "start_url": "/",
  "display": "standalone",
  "background_color": "#1E293B",
  "theme_color": "#3B82F6",
  "icons": [
    {
      "src": "/icons/icon-192.png",
      "sizes": "192x192",
      "type": "image/png",
      "purpose": "any maskable"
    },
    {
      "src": "/icons/icon-512.png",
      "sizes": "512x512",
      "type": "image/png",
      "purpose": "any maskable"
    }
  ],
  "categories": ["business", "productivity"],
  "orientation": "any",
  "scope": "/",
  "lang": "en",
  "dir": "ltr"
}
```

### 10.2 Service Worker Strategy

```javascript
// Caching strategies
const strategies = {
  // App shell - cache first
  shell: 'CacheFirst',

  // API data - stale while revalidate
  api: 'StaleWhileRevalidate',

  // Images - cache first with expiration
  images: 'CacheFirst',

  // Real-time data - network first
  realtime: 'NetworkFirst'
};
```

### 10.3 Install Prompt

```
┌────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                                      × │
│  📱 Add to Home Screen                 │
│                                        │
│  Install the RSMM app for quick       │
│  access and offline support.          │
│                                        │
│  [Not Now]              [Install]     │
└────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

---

## 11. Performance Optimization

### 11.1 Performance Budgets

| Metric | Target | Mobile 3G |
|--------|--------|-----------|
| First Contentful Paint | <1.5s | <3s |
| Largest Contentful Paint | <2.5s | <4s |
| Time to Interactive | <3.5s | <7s |
| Total Blocking Time | <200ms | <600ms |
| Cumulative Layout Shift | <0.1 | <0.1 |
| Bundle size (gzipped) | <200KB | <200KB |

### 11.2 Optimization Techniques

| Technique | Implementation |
|-----------|----------------|
| Code splitting | Route-based lazy loading |
| Image optimization | WebP format, srcset |
| Critical CSS | Inline above-fold styles |
| Prefetching | Priority routes |
| Skeleton screens | Show immediately while loading |
| Virtual scrolling | For long lists (128 trains) |

### 11.3 Skeleton Loading States

```
┌────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ ████████████████                       │
│ Kinki Sharyo                           │
├────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ ████████   │ ████████████  │ ████      │
│            │               │           │
├────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ ████████████████████████               │
│ ████████████████████                   │
├────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ ████████████        ████████████       │
└────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

---

## 12. Accessibility (Mobile)

### 12.1 Screen Reader Support

```html
<!-- Train card -->
<article
  role="article"
  aria-label="Train KS-045, Kinki Sharyo fleet, 95% PM window, Warning status"
>
  <h3 id="train-title-045">KS-045</h3>
  <p aria-describedby="train-title-045">Kinki Sharyo</p>

  <div role="meter" aria-valuenow="95" aria-valuemin="0" aria-valuemax="100">
    PM Window: 95%
  </div>

  <button aria-label="View details for train KS-045">
    View Details
  </button>
</article>
```

### 12.2 Focus Management

- Trap focus in modals/bottom sheets
- Return focus to trigger element on close
- Visible focus indicators (4px outline)
- Skip-to-content link

### 12.3 Reduced Motion

```css
@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) {
  *,
  *::before,
  *::after {
    animation-duration: 0.01ms !important;
    animation-iteration-count: 1 !important;
    transition-duration: 0.01ms !important;
  }
}
```

---

## 13. Testing Checklist

### 13.1 Device Testing Matrix

| Device | OS | Browser | Priority |
|--------|----|---------|---------|
| iPhone 14 Pro | iOS 17 | Safari | High |
| iPhone SE | iOS 17 | Safari | High |
| iPad Pro 12.9" | iPadOS 17 | Safari | High |
| Samsung Galaxy S23 | Android 14 | Chrome | High |
| Pixel 7 | Android 14 | Chrome | Medium |
| iPad Air | iPadOS 17 | Safari | Medium |
| Samsung Tab S9 | Android 14 | Chrome | Medium |

### 13.2 Test Scenarios

| Scenario | Test Method |
|----------|-------------|
| Offline functionality | Airplane mode toggle |
| Slow network | Chrome DevTools throttling |
| Touch targets | Manual testing with fingers |
| Orientation change | Device rotation |
| Keyboard navigation | External keyboard |
| Screen reader | VoiceOver / TalkBack |
| PWA install | Add to home screen |
| Background sync | Network restoration |

---

## 14. Gestalt Principles (Mobile)

### 14.1 Proximity
- Group related actions in bottom sheets
- Cluster form fields logically
- Maintain consistent spacing

### 14.2 Similarity
- Consistent card styling across views
- Uniform icon language
- Same color coding for status

### 14.3 Enclosure
- Cards with clear boundaries
- Bottom sheet modal frames
- Section separators

### 14.4 Continuity
- Smooth scroll animations
- Gesture continuity (swipe flow)
- Consistent navigation direction

### 14.5 Figure-Ground
- Elevated bottom sheets (shadow)
- Modal overlay dimming
- Active tab highlighting

### 14.6 Common Fate
- List items scroll together
- Synchronized pull-to-refresh
- Batch selection animations

---

## 15. Unknown Unknowns (Risks & Considerations)

### Identified During Design

1. **Data usage concerns**: Field workers may have limited data plans
2. **Battery consumption**: Background sync impact on battery
3. **Device fragmentation**: Android device diversity
4. **Network reliability**: Rural/underground coverage gaps
5. **PWA limitations**: iOS restrictions on background processing
6. **Biometric auth**: Device-specific implementation differences
7. **Camera quality**: QR scanning in poor lighting
8. **Screen size extremes**: Very large tablets, foldables
9. **Rugged device support**: Industrial-grade tablets
10. **Multi-window support**: Split-screen on tablets
11. **Handedness**: Left-handed users and navigation
12. **Accessibility zoom**: Text scaling impact on layout
13. **Dark mode sync**: System vs app preference
14. **Update mechanism**: PWA update prompts
15. **Storage limits**: Browser cache limits
16. **Cross-device sync**: Session continuity
17. **Print from mobile**: Report printing support
18. **External keyboard**: Tablet with keyboard use case
19. **Landscape lock**: Specific views that need landscape
20. **Deep linking**: Navigation from push notifications

---

## 16. Implementation Checklist

### Phase 1: Foundation
- [ ] Set up responsive breakpoints
- [ ] Implement mobile navigation
- [ ] Create base card components
- [ ] Set up PWA manifest

### Phase 2: Core Features
- [ ] Mobile dashboard view
- [ ] Train list (cards)
- [ ] Train detail view
- [ ] Basic offline support

### Phase 3: Field Worker
- [ ] Quick check-in flow
- [ ] Mileage entry (offline)
- [ ] Task list view
- [ ] QR code scanning

### Phase 4: Advanced
- [ ] Full offline sync
- [ ] Push notifications
- [ ] Background sync
- [ ] Role-based views

### Phase 5: Polish
- [ ] Performance optimization
- [ ] Accessibility audit
- [ ] Device testing
- [ ] PWA install flow

---

## 17. Change Log

| Version | Date | Changes |
|---------|------|---------|
| 1.0 | 2026-01-08 | Initial specification |

---

*Document prepared for MILEAGE-UX-010*
*Rolling Stock Mileage Management System*

content.text:
Perfect! I've successfully read all 9 UX specification files. Now let me compile a comprehensive report of the UX specifications and check which React components exist.

## UX SPECIFICATIONS EXPLORATION REPORT

I've successfully reviewed all 9 completed UX specification documents for the Rolling Stock Mileage Management System. Here's a comprehensive summary:

---

## DOCUMENT SUMMARY

### 1. **MILEAGE-UX-003: Centralised Dashboard**
- **Main Components:**
  - Hero KPI Cards (4 metrics: Fleet Availability, PM Compliance, Resource Utilisation, Cost Variance)
  - Fleet Status Panel (by fleet type breakdown)
  - Alerts & Warnings Panel
  - PM Schedule Timeline (30-day view)
  - Resource Utilisation Chart
  - Today's Run Assignments table
- **Implementation Status:** `Dashboard.tsx` exists in `/src/frontend/pages/`

---

### 2. **MILEAGE-UX-004: Train Mileage Status Grid**
- **Main Components:**
  - Data Grid with sticky headers/columns
  - Filter Bar (Fleet, Status, PM Due, Search)
  - Summary Bar (counts by status)
  - Train status columns (ID, Mileage, PM Window, Fitness Status, Next PM, Actions)
  - Pagination controls (25 rows default)
  - Bulk action bar
- **Key Features:** Virtual scrolling, TanStack Table integration, 128 train capacity
- **Implementation Status:** `TrainGrid.tsx` exists in `/src/frontend/pages/`

---

### 3. **MILEAGE-UX-005: Run Assignment Recommendation UI**
- **Main Components:**
  - Daily Schedule View (3-column layout)
  - Fleet Overview Panel
  - Train Pool Panel (draggable)
  - Assignment Board (center workspace)
  - Recommendations Panel (AI suggestions)
  - Mileage Balance Visualization
  - Weekly Planner View
  - Operations-Maintenance Messaging
- **Key Features:** Drag-and-drop assignments, AI recommendation scores, fleet balance tracking
- **Implementation Status:** `Assignments.tsx` exists in `/src/frontend/pages/`

---

### 4. **MILEAGE-UX-002: Man-Hour Projection Chart**
- **Main Components:**
  - Stacked Area/Line Chart (Availability + Adjusted Workload + Predicted Maintenance)
  - Data Table below chart (showing task counts, man-hours, gaps)
  - Timeline Controls (date range selector, quick ranges)
  - Comparison Mode Toggle (Without/With Adjustment)
- **Key Features:** 12-year contract period visualization, gap analysis coloring
- **Implementation Status:** Part of Dashboard or Reports module (no dedicated page found yet)

---

### 5. **MILEAGE-UX-006: Maintenance Compliance Dashboard**
- **Main Components:**
  - Executive Summary (4 KPI cards + trend + breakdown + alerts)
  - Schedule Compliance View (PM rates, backlog, overdue analysis)
  - Cost Compliance View (budget vs actual, variance waterfall, category breakdown)
  - Mileage Compliance View (planned vs actual, fleet balance, deviation heatmap)
  - Compliance Reports View (standard, scheduled, custom)
- **Implementation Status:** Not yet implemented as dedicated page

---

### 6. **MILEAGE-UX-007: What-If Scenario / Simulation UI**
- **Main Components:**
  - Scenario Manager (template library, scenario list)
  - Scenario Builder (parameter sliders, real-time preview)
  - Scenario Comparison (side-by-side, overlay, table views)
  - Results Explorer (timeline projections, resource impact, cost projections, risk analysis)
  - Sensitivity Analysis (tornado chart, Monte Carlo distribution)
- **Key Features:** 6 quick-start templates, parameter sliders with real-time feedback
- **Implementation Status:** `Simulations.tsx` exists in `/src/frontend/pages/`

---

### 7. **MILEAGE-UX-008: Report Generation Templates**
- **Main Components:**
  - Standard Report Library (6 categories, 20+ report types)
  - Report Generation Modal (parameters, format selection)
  - Scheduled Reports (frequency, distribution, archive)
  - Custom Report Builder (drag-drop components, data binding)
  - Report History (search, filtering, retention policy)
- **Key Features:** 6 report categories, multi-format export (PDF, Excel, CSV, HTML, JSON)
- **Implementation Status:** `Reports.tsx` exists in `/src/frontend/pages/`

---

### 8. **MILEAGE-UX-009: Alert & Notification System**
- **Main Components:**
  - Toast Notifications (transient, auto-dismiss)
  - Inline Notifications (contextual, dismissible)
  - Actionable Notifications (require response)
  - Modal Notifications (blocking)
  - Notification Center Panel
  - Full Notification List View
- **Key Features:** 4 priority levels (P1-P4), escalation workflow (4 tiers), delivery channels (in-app, push, email, SMS)
- **Implementation Status:** Core notification infrastructure needed

---

### 9. **MILEAGE-UX-010: Mobile Responsive Design**
- **Main Components:**
  - Mobile Navigation (bottom tab bar + hamburger menu)
  - Responsive Layouts (6 breakpoints: xs, sm, md, lg, xl, xxl)
  - Card-based Layout (replaces tables on mobile)
  - Offline-First Design (service worker caching, optimistic updates)
  - Role-based Mobile Views (Operations, Maintenance, Management)
  - PWA Configuration
- **Key Features:** 48px touch targets, offline data sync, field worker optimizations
- **Implementation Status:** Mobile support infrastructure exists in codebase

---

## FRONTEND PAGES INVENTORY

### Currently Implemented (6 pages):
1. **Dashboard.tsx** - Maps to MILEAGE-UX-003 (Centralised Dashboard)
2. **TrainGrid.tsx** - Maps to MILEAGE-UX-004 (Train Mileage Status Grid)
3. **Assignments.tsx** - Maps to MILEAGE-UX-005 (Run Assignment Recommendation UI)
4. **Simulations.tsx** - Maps to MILEAGE-UX-007 (What-If Scenario / Simulation)
5. **Reports.tsx** - Maps to MILEAGE-UX-008 (Report Generation)
6. **Planning.tsx** - (Purpose: likely related to scheduling/PM planning)

### Not Yet Implemented as Dedicated Pages:
- **Compliance Dashboard** (MILEAGE-UX-006) - Should be a dedicated page or part of Dashboard tabs
- **Alerts/Notifications** (MILEAGE-UX-009) - System-wide, not a page but infrastructure
- **Mobile Responsive** (MILEAGE-UX-010) - Responsive adaptation of above pages

---

## IMPLEMENTATION MAPPING

| Document ID | Title | React Page | Status | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| MILEAGE-UX-002 | Man-Hour Projection Chart | Dashboard/Reports | Partial | High |
| MILEAGE-UX-003 | Centralised Dashboard | Dashboard.tsx | ✓ Exists | Complete |
| MILEAGE-UX-004 | Train Mileage Grid | TrainGrid.tsx | ✓ Exists | Complete |
| MILEAGE-UX-005 | Run Assignment UI | Assignments.tsx | ✓ Exists | Complete |
| MILEAGE-UX-006 | Maintenance Compliance | *Create New* | ❌ Missing | High |
| MILEAGE-UX-007 | What-If Scenarios | Simulations.tsx | ✓ Exists | Complete |
| MILEAGE-UX-008 | Report Generation | Reports.tsx | ✓ Exists | Complete |
| MILEAGE-UX-009 | Alert/Notification System | *Infrastructure* | ❌ Missing | High |
| MILEAGE-UX-010 | Mobile Responsive Design | *All pages* | Partial | Medium |

---

## KEY INSIGHTS

### Components That Need Implementation:
1. **Compliance Dashboard page** - Multi-tab view with Executive, Schedule, Cost, Mileage, and Reports sections
2. **Notification Center** - Bell icon, dropdown panel, full notification list view
3. **Alert Management System** - Toast, inline, actionable, and modal alert components
4. **Notification Preferences** - User settings for channels, quiet hours, digests, subscriptions

### Design Patterns Used:
- Gestalt principles (Proximity, Similarity, Enclosure, Continuity, Figure-Ground, Common Fate)
- Role-based customization (5 user roles with different default widgets)
- Real-time data refresh strategies (WebSocket for fleet status, 5-min intervals for most data)
- Responsive design (6 breakpoints, mobile-first approach)
- Accessibility compliance (WCAG 2.1 AA, full keyboard navigation, screen reader support)

### Technology Recommendations (from specs):
- **Frontend:** React + TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Headless UI
- **Charts:** Apache ECharts or Recharts
- **Tables:** TanStack Table (Vue/React Table)
- **State Management:** TanStack Query for server state
- **Real-time:** Socket.io for WebSocket
- **PWA:** Service Workers, Firebase Cloud Messaging (FCM)

---

All 9 UX specification files have been thoroughly reviewed and documented. The codebase has 6 main pages implemented, with **Compliance Dashboard** and **Alert/Notification System** being the primary missing components identified by the specifications.
